Even without his cyborg functions and an arsenal of weaponry, Franky is a highly skilled boxer, which, when combined with his cybernetically enhanced physical strength, enables him to defeat highly skilled and dangerously

The Toshi Toshi no Mi is a Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to manipulate the ages of people or objects, making them an Age Manipulation Human ( 年齢自在人間 , Nenrei Jizai Ningen ) . As part of an experiment, Jaygarcia Saturn administered an extract of the fruit to Jewelry Bonney when she was an infant. Etymology Toshi ( 年, 歳 ) is Japanese for "age" or "year". Strengths The main strength of the fruit is that it allows the user to freely manipulate the aging process of anything, including themselves, other people, animals and objects. A unique effect of this ability is that the years the user steals from people are manifested in the form of sparkling jewelry and shapes. The user can employ this power to disguise themselves, as well as cripple opponents by rapidly aging their intended targets into their senior years or regressing them into small, weak children. As such, this ability is noted to be extremely powerful because it merely needs an instance of physical contact (directly or through an user-wielded object) for its effects to apply. The user can also force a target's body to age into a skeleton, but only for a short time, thus traumatizing them with a near-death experience. Even people with knowledge of Haki , such as Marine Vice Admirals , will become helpless if they end up falling to an age regression. Meanwhile, objects can corrode and eventually disintegrate into dust following a rapid progression of their time, giving the fruit further utility as a way to easily break into sealed off locations. Any target whose bodily age has been altered by this power still retains all of their memories and cognitive abilities, as shown by the Marines and Bonney herself acting the same as normal, regardless of the age their body is showing them to be (though targets that have been reduced to toddlers have a tendency to cry as such). However, it has been non-canonically shown that a target that had been aged from an infant state to an adult state can gain knowledge based on whatever the target is, such as when Bonney aged a clutch of eggs into a flock of full-grown birds to instantly knew how to fly. In addition, any markings or bodily modifications the target may have will not be affected by this power, as Jinbe still retained his scar around his left eye after being turned into a child by this ability, and Vegapunk retained his sliced-off upper head and attached structure. Similarly, the progress of any disease the target has will not change if they are turned into an older version of their present self, even if they were likely to die long before reaching that age. The true strength of the fruit is that the user can transform into any possible future forms while aging themselves or others forward in time, regardless of physical limitations. The user can, with enough imagination, warp their body around to attain the forms of muscular warriors, different race , or even a different Devil Fruit class, notably the Awakened Form of the Hito Hito no Mi, Model: Nika of the Zoan category, if they are somehow able to envision it as an actual possibility. Children in particular are implied to highly benefit from this specific application of the fruit's powers, thanks to their highly imaginative and impressionable minds. In the anime, the user glows pink while using their powers. The manga, meanwhile, has no other visual indication of the ability being employed besides the obvious physical changes that come with different ages. Weaknesses The fruit's main weakness is that its aging effects, when applied to living things, are temporary, meaning victims will eventually revert to their rightful age at some point. While the user can remain in their adult form for long periods of time and even while knocked out, it has to be continuously maintained; overexertions, such as intense battles, can dispel the self-applied effect and revert back to their real age. It is unknown if the user has any sort of control over the duration of their ability's effect or if they are capable of immediately reversing it after a prior application. Additionally, targets with a very long lifespan are less affected by the effects of age manipulation. Thus Bonney could not weaken Jaygarcia Saturn , who has not physically aged in at least 200 years. While the fruit allows the user to transform into any future as long as they can imagine it, the more the user is aware of the reality, the narrower the future possibility becomes; thus, futures based on faith will be weakened by doubts. A clever enough enemy can exploit this weakness by psychologically tormenting the user and feeding into their hesitations. Aside from this, the user suffers the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. It is implied that submerging the user might cause the Devil Fruit's effects to be nullified, or at least take away enough of the user's energy to maintain them active. Bonney reverted to her true age while she was swept up by the sea before being rescued by the Straw Hat Pirates . Notably, the user's clothes will not change in accord with their modified age, unlike how it usually happens with other Devil Fruit powers. Usage Bonney's standard transformation. Bonney's elderly transformation. Bonney mainly uses her ability to maintain the appearance of a woman in her early 20s in public. By shifting her apparent age up or down, she is able to fool people unaware of her powers into believing that she is somebody other than herself. She has been shown doing this in order to pretend to be related to someone (like how she did at the Sabaody Archipelago , by lying to Charlos about being Roronoa Zoro 's little sister), steal food or to infiltrate restricted locations, all under the guise of someone else. At first, Bonney had trouble figuring out how her fruit worked, and needed some time to master her new abilities. By using Queen Conney as a model, Bonney quickly learned how to alter her own appearance into that of an elderly woman, which was enough to fool many onlookers, including her own father. During the period of time where she was supervised by Alpha , Bonney was instructed by Conney to keep her abilities a secret just in case. This would later prove a useful decision to allow Bonney to escape the World Government 's watch. For combat, Bonney tends to employ her abilities on enemies by changing their age to extreme highs or lows, making them too old or young to engage in battle. Bonney is also able to tap into alternate futures with her forward-aging techniques, in case said futures provide a physical boost to her normal abilities. Thanks to her own vivid imagination and the legends her father used to tell her, Bonney can somehow mimic even the powers of Nika , a holy figure, by shapeshifting herself into a future form that is capable of inflating her arms and using them for attacks, akin to the effects of Luffy 's Gear 3 . Like many Devil Fruit users, Bonney can channel her abilities through foreign objects that she currently wields. She has been seen utilizing age-changing effects through both a pipe weapon and a pistol. This complements Bonney's skillset really well, as she has no dedicated weapon and tends to pick up whatever she can find around for combat. Techniques Distortion Future ( 歪んだ未来 , Disutōshon Fyūchā ) : Bonney ages herself up into a possible future version. The standard version of this move grants Bonney a large and muscular physique, similar to her adopted father 's, becoming physically stronger as a result (enough to destroy metallic walls with ease). Bonney can also apply this technique onto others as she did to Monkey D. Luffy at Egghead 's scrapyard, turning him into a 70-year-old man who was stated to come from a "different future". This move was properly named during Bonney's confrontation with Vegapunk. In the English adaptations, this is called Distorted Future . Distortion Future: Nika Mitaina Mirai ( ゆがんだ未来 “ニカ”みたいな未来 , Disutōshon Fyūchā: Nika Mitaina Mirai , literally meaning "Distortion Future: A Future Like Nika") : Bonney ages herself up into a possible future version, gaining a physique she imagined like Nika , and being able to stretch and enlarge her arm similar to both Nika’s rubbery properties and the Gear 3 form of the Gomu Gomu no Mi . Bonney also utilized Nika's power to gain a muscular physique that allowed her to enlarge her arm and compress her fist, which follows the same concept as Gear 4 . This was first used against Alpha . In the English adaptations, this is called Distorted Future: Nika-Like Future . Distortion Future: Kyojin Mitaina Mirai ( ゆがんだ未来 巨人みたいな未来 , Disutōshon Fyūchā: Kyojin Mitaina Mirai , literally meaning "Distortion Future: A Future Like a Giant") : Bonney ages herself up into a possible future version, gaining an incredibly large physique akin to that of a giant . This was first used against Ethanbaron V. Nusjuro . By Bonney's own account, this form cannot be maintained for very long. In the VIZ manga, this is called Distorted Future: Giant-Like Future . Distortion Future: Atashi Ga Ichiban Jiyuna Mirai ( ゆがんだ未来 あたしが一番自由な未来 , Disutōshon Fyūchā: Atashi Ga Ichiban Jiyūna Mirai , literally meaning "Distortion Future: A Future Where I am Most Free") : An upgraded version of Distortion Future: Nika Mitaina Mirai . Bonney ages herself up into a possible future version, gaining a transformation akin to that of the awakened form of the Gomu Gomu no Mi, as Luffy described his own form as having the most freedom. The technique was first used after Luffy encouraged her to help him fight the Five Elders. This form is very taxing, as Bonney cannot maintain it for long and is left completely exhausted afterwards. In the English adaptations, this is called Distorted Future: Future Where I'm The Most Free . Tokudai!! "Nika no Punch" ( 特大!! "ニカのパンチ" , Tokudai!! "Nika no Panchi" , literally meaning "Extra Large!! 'Nika Punch'") : Bonney inflates her fist and covers it with Armament Haki before launching it forward in a punch. This technique essentially follows the same concept as Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Elephant Gun using Gear 3, having seen and copied the way Luffy utilized Gear 5 in his fight against Jaygarcia Saturn , with the only slight difference being that Bonney's Armament Haki only covers the front half of her arm. This technique was first used to enhance Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Dawn Balloon attack on Marcus Mars on Egghead . In the English adaptations, this is called Super-Size!! Nika Punch . Kaiho no Nika Punch ( 解放のニカパンチ , Kaihō no Nika Panchi , literally meaning "Nika Punch of Liberation") : A combination attack performed by Luffy using Gear 5 and Gomu Gomu no Gigant and Bonney while assuming her Nika form. While assuming his gigantic, inflated body, Luffy attacks the enemy from every direction with a barrage of Armament Haki -enhanced punches, while Bonney strikes the enemy right down the middle with her Tokudai!! "Nika no Punch" . This was first used against Jaygarcia Saturn . In the English adaptations, this is called Liberation Nika Punch . Toshi Tsuki ( 跡死 突き , Toshi Tsuki , literally meaning "Trace Death Thrust") : While wielding a staff weapon or another object, Bonney thrusts it into a target, aging them into a younger version of themselves. The target's lost years are manifested in the form of glittering jewels of various shapes that are expelled from their body and then scattered around. Bonney first used this on Vegapunk , turning him into a helpless child. The name of the technique is a pun on toshitsuki (年月) meaning "months and years". In the English adaptations, this is called Age Skewer . Oil Shock ( 老化衝撃 , Oiru Shokku , literally meaning "Aging Shock") : Bonney applies her aging effects to a metallic object, corroding and then breaking it. She first used this to destroy a door lock leading to a room in Vegapunk's laboratory. The name of the technique is a pun on 老いる ( oiru , meaning "to age" ) . In the English adaptations, this is called Oiled Shock . NDE ( 臨死体験 , Enu Dī Ī , literally meaning "Near-Death Experience") : Bonney pulls out a pistol and fires a projectile against the enemy. Rather than sustaining physical injury, the target is trapped in a harrowing illusion in which their body appears to wither away into a bare skeleton. The effects of the illusion, by the time it wears off shortly after, leaves them with the psychological trauma of them narrowly escaping death. Bonney first used this move against a group of Marine soldiers. NDE is an acronym for " near-death experience ." History While Bonney was a baby in Mary Geoise , Jaygarcia Saturn performed an experiment on her to test whether a Devil Fruit could be given to an infant via an extract. While Bonney acquired the power, the fruit itself was rendered useless. Following Sorbet Kingdom 's Solo Revolution at age 6, Bonney subconsciously accessed the fruit's power in the church she was confined in, leading to herself becoming confused at its power, as well as her adoptive father mistaking Bonney for her late mother Ginny . It was only during the Egghead Incident did Saturn reveal to Bonney how he granted her said power.

The Basu Basu no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to turn anything they touch into a bomb , making them a Bomb Transformation Human ( 爆弾変化人間 , Bakudan Henka Ningen ) . It was eaten by Balzac . Etymology Strengths and Weaknesses The main strength of this Devil Fruit is that it allows the user to turn anything they touch into a bomb . Aside from the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses, there seems to be no known weaknesses to this power.

As an adult, Sabo is shown to have extraordinary agility as he easily dodged an attack from a Fighting Fish, as well as tremendous physical strength, clashing with Jesus Burgess, a pirate known for

Main Geppo Tekkai Rankyaku Soru Shigan Kami-e Rokuogan Shigan is one of the Six Powers that allows one to use their finger as a weapon. Overview Shigan is a close-quarter combat technique, in which the user uses their finger to pierce the target's flesh, at a very high speed. The resulting wound is roughly the size of a bullet wound. It is a very useful technique that can be carried out rapidly and accurately, which works well with Six Powers users' impressive speed and agile techniques. The technique can be rendered even stronger if the user coats their finger with Armament Haki. This technique can be enhanced by Zoan users if their Human-Beast forms grants them sharp claws. Lucci, Jabra, Dalmatian, and Who's-Who all have Human-Beast forms which allow them to stab their opponents with even sharper claws, now akin to modern bullets. Also, every CP9 member who knows this technique has demonstrated their own variant techniques of Shigan, aside from Blueno. However, as the finger is a very weak body part, the user can risk breaking their fingers from this technique, especially if the opponent can harden their bodies with Armament Haki. Also, this technique is ineffective against eaters of the Gomu Gomu no Mi, as the human finger is not sharp enough to pierce the opponent's rubber body. Variations Shigan "Oren" ( 指銃「 黄蓮 」 , Shigan "Ōren" , literally meaning "Finger Gun: 'Yellow Lotus'") : Used by Lucci. This attack is simply a rapid-fire version of the normal Shigan technique that hits the enemy multiple times. In the anime, he is also seen using this technique to kill the captain of a crew who raided the kingdom 15 years ago, but did not name it as "Oren". This is called Finger Pistol Yellow Lotus in the VIZ manga and Funimation dub and Shigan: Yellow Lotus in One Piece: Unlimited Adventure (dubbed by Funimation). In the English localization of One Piece: Burning Blood , it is mistakenly translated as Tempest Kick: Yellow Lotus . Tobu Shigan ( 飛ぶ 指銃 , Tobu Shigan , literally meaning "Flying Finger Gun") : Used by Stussy . A projectile version of Shigan that fires a sharp compressed air shockwave from the finger thrust enabling the user to injure someone without getting close. In the English adaptations, this is called Flying Finger Pistol . Tobu Shigan "Bachi" ( 飛ぶ指銃「 撥 」 , Tobu Shigan "Bachi" , literally meaning "Flying Finger Gun: 'Plectrum'") : Used by Lucci in his Human-Beast form. Similar to "Rankyaku", this technique is able to pierce Luffy's rubber body. Unlike all other versions, this type of Shigan is launched by flicking instead of jabbing, though in the anime, Lucci and Jabra demonstrated a variant that involves jabbing. This is called Flying Finger Pistol Sting in the VIZ manga and Funimation dub and Flying Finger Pistol Pick in the Funimation subs. The attack's name is a possible play on the term hachi/bachi ( 蜂 , lit. "bee") , hence the projectile nature of the attack and the English translation. Hagan ( 刃銃 , Hagan , literally meaning "Blade Gun") : Used by Who's-Who . He thrusts forward his dagger repeatedly, launching a series of sharp, compressed air projectiles. The attack was first used against Jinbe, who deflected it using Fish-Man Karate . In the English adaptations, this is called Blade Pistol . Gagan ( 牙銃 , Gagan , literally meaning "Fang Gun") : Used by Who's-Who in his Human-Beast form. Who's-Who quickly thrusts his maw forward, releasing a blast of compressed air that is shaped like his fangs. The blast makes a biting motion once it connects to a surface, gouging a clean, round hole out of the struck target. It was first used against Jinbe, who successfully dodged the first attack. In the English adaptations, this is called Fang Pistol . Shigan "Madara" ( 指銃「 斑 」 , Shigan "Madara" , literally meaning "Finger Gun: 'Speckles'") : Used by Lucci and Who's-Who, in their Human-Beast forms. Appears as Oren , but is performed with both hands instead of one and in Human-Beast Form (meaning that his fingers are clawed), this makes for the ultimate Shigan barrage. This is called Finger Pistol Spots in the English adaptations and Shigan: Spots in One Piece: Unlimited Adventure (dubbed by Funimation). In One Piece: Pirate Warriors 3 and 4 , Lucci is able to (and can only) perform this in his human form. Bigan ( 鼻銃 , Bigan , literally meaning "Nose Gun") : Used by Kaku, in his Human-Beast Form. In this attack, Kaku uses his nose instead of his finger to perform the attack. As his nose is much larger in diameter than a finger, the hole it creates is also much larger than that of a normal Shigan , as well as being completely square. This is called Nose Pistol in the English adaptations and Shigan: Nose Gun in One Piece: Unlimited Adventure (dubbed by Funimation). Kyoku Bigan "Kirimanjaro" ( 極・鼻銃「 麒麟 マン 射櫓 」 , Kyōku Bigan "Kirimanjaro" , literally meaning "Extreme Nose Gun: 'Giraffe-Man Shooting Oar'") : Used by Kaku in Human-Beast Form. After using Kirin Hodai to completely retract his neck into his body, he can fire it out like a cannon, making his Bigan much stronger. The name Kirimanjaro is a reference to "Kilimanjaro", the tallest mountain in Africa that shares the same name. In the English adaptations, this is called Extreme Nose Pistol Giraffe Blast . Jusshigan ( 十指銃 , Jusshigan , literally meaning "Ten Finger Guns") : Used by Bonham and Jabra in his Human-Beast Form. In this attack, the user puts both of his hands together and performs Shigan with all ten of his fingers at once, greatly damaging the opponent. In the English adaptations, this is called Ten Finger Pistol . Gekko Jusshigan ( 月光十指銃 , Gekkō Jusshigan , literally meaning "Moonlight Ten Finger Guns") : A combination of Jusshigan and Geppo . After using Geppo to get himself into the air and subsequently avoid his foe's attack, Jabra finally uses it to launch himself towards his airborne target in a vertical drop. The added momentum and the vulnerable target makes the following Jusshigan more effective, and harder to avoid. In the English adaptations, this is called Moonlight Ten Finger Pistol . Shigan Cue ( 指銃 Q , Shigan Kyū , literally meaning "Finger Gun: Cue") : Used by Kumadori. In this attack, Kumadori uses his blunt staff in the same way a finger is normally used in Shigan . The Q in the name is a reference to a pool cue, which is held in the same manner that Kumadori wields his staff. In the English adaptations, this is called Finger Pistol Q . Shishi Shigan ( 獅子指銃 , Shishi Shigan , literally meaning "Lion Finger Gun") : Used by Kumadori. Rather than using his own fingers, Kumadori shapes his hair into the form of hands and repeatedly uses the fingers of said hands to rapidly jab the opponent, similar to Lucci's Shigan "Madara" , but less powerful. In the English adaptations, this is called Shishi Finger Pistol . Jugon ( 獣厳 , Jūgon , literally meaning "Stern Beast" or "Beast Stern") : Used by Fukurou, this is a very strong punch which is done at the same speed as Shigan , making it even more powerful. In the English adaptations, this is called Solid Beast . Jugon Ogi: "Fukuro Dataki" ( 獣厳「奥義」" 梟叩 き" , Jūgon Ōgi: "Fukurō Dataki" , literally meaning "Stern Beast Secret Technique: 'Owl Thrasher'") : A rapid-fire version of Jugon , in which Fukurou unleashes a flurry of punches at the enemy while using Soru to move around him, in order to strike from all directions. In the anime, Fukurou creates after images of himself that circle the opponent to strike them. This is called Solid Beast Supreme Technique Owl Strike in the VIZ manga and Secret Technique: Owl Thrashing in the Funimation adaptations. The kanji for Fukurodataki is a play on the original kanji 袋叩き (lit. "Pouch Thrasher"), which is read the same way (or as Fukurotataki with a "t" instead) and is meant to be a term for beating up someone via a gang up. Shinaru Shigan "Whip" ( しなる指銃「 鞭 」 , Shinaru Shigan "Uippu" , literally meaning "Bending Finger Gun: 'Whip'") : Used by Kalifa . In this attack, Kalifa pulls back her arm as if to use a normal Shigan attack, then whips her arm out at the opponent at high speed, causing her arm to appear as if it is bending like a whip would, and hits the enemy with her finger. The whip aspect of this variation results in the added effect of the target being thrown forcibly backwards. This is called Bendy Finger Pistol Whip in the VIZ manga and Supple Finger Pistol Whip in the Funimation adaptations. Shugan ( 手銃 , Shugan , literally meaning "Hand Gun") : Used by Lucci in his awakened Human-Hybrid form. Lucci keeps all five fingers extended and stabs the opponent with his whole hand. This was first used against Sentomaru . In the English adaptations, this is called Hand Pistol . Shugan: Oren ( 手銃 黄蓮 , Shugan: Ōren , literally meaning Hand Gun: Yellow Lotus) : Used by Lucci in his Human-Hybrid form. Lucci uses a rapid-fire version of Shugan to hit his opponent several times similar to Shigan "Oren" . This was first used against S-Bear . In the English adaptations, this is called Hand Pistol Yellow Lotus . Shugan: Madara ( 手銃 斑 , Shugan: Madara , literally meaning "Hand Gun: Speckles") : Used by Lucci in his awakened Human-Hybrid form. Lucci performs Shugan with both hands in a very rapid barrage of attacks similar to Shigan "Madara" . This was first used against Zoro . In the English adaptations, this is called Hand Pistol Spots . Tosa Gami ( 土茶 噛み , Tosa Gami , literally meaning "Earthen Tea Bite") : Used by Tosa . After hardening his hands with Armament Haki , Tosa holds his hands together and performs Shigan with all ten fingers, similar to Jabra's Jusshigan . Tosa claims it can pierce any armor. This was first attempted to be used against Jewelry Bonney , but Tosa was stopped by Brogy before it could connect. In the English adaptations, this is called Tosa Bite . Non-Canon Variations Tobu Shigan "Mitsubachi" ( 飛ぶ指銃「三撥」 , Tobu Shigan "Mitsubachi" , literally meaning "Flying Finger Gun: 'Three Plectrums'") : Used by Lucci in his Human-Beast form. Lucci fires the Tobu Shigan: Bachi three times consecutively. This is called Three Picks in the Funimation subs and Rapid Fire Triple Sting in the dub. The kanji for mitsubachi is a play on 蜜蜂, which is the Japanese term for a honeybee. Tobu Shigan "Hibachi" ( 飛ぶ指銃「火撥」 , Tobu Shigan "Hibachi" , literally meaning "Flying Finger Gun: 'Fire Plectrum'") : Used by Lucci in his Human-Beast form. Same as Tobu Shigan: Bachi , except that the projectile is a fireball, possibly created through friction, that burns the victim upon impact. Hibachi is a type of Japanese grill, or a brazier (and is originally read with the kanji 火鉢). This is called Flying Finger Pistol Fire Pick in the Funimation subs, Flying Finger Pistol Fire Sting in the dub, and Finger Pistol: Fire Sting in the English localization of One Piece: Burning Blood . Shigan "Five" ( 指銃「ファイブ」 , Shigan "Faibu" , literally meaning "Finger Gun: 'Five'") : Used by Shuzo, he does Shigan with all five fingers on one hand. This was first used against Luffy. In the Funimation adaptations, this is called Finger Pistol Five . Shigan "Ten" ( 指銃「テン」 , Shigan "Ten" , literally meaning "Finger Gun: 'Ten'") : Used by Shuzo, he does Shigan with all ten fingers on both hands similar to Jabra's Jusshigan. This was first used against Luffy. In the Funimation adaptations, this is called Finger Pistol Ten . Shigan "Sentoso" ( 指銃「仙洞草」 , Shigan "Sentōsō" , literally meaning "Finger Gun: 'Hermit Cave Grass'") : An upward version of Shigan: Oren used by Lucci in One Piece: Gigant Battle! 2 New World , as a followup to his Shoukoushou ( 昇硬掌 , lit. "Rising Stiff Palm") (his upward weak attack).

The Goe Goe no Mi is a movie-only Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to create beams of sound that look like energy beams by shouting. It was eaten by Eldoraggo , the main antagonist in the first movie . Etymology "Goe" is a variation of "koe" ( 声 ) , meaning "voice" in Japanese. Strengths and Weaknesses The main strength of this fruit is that it allows the user to create powerful beams of concentrated sound energy that can destroy anything in its path. These beams can be used to either attack enemies or dig through the ground. The strength of these beams can be controlled. Though the beams are powerful, they are not volatile enough to explode should they strike a malleable surface, as shown by Monkey D. Luffy , who was able to collect the blast with his rubber powers and reflect it back at Eldoraggo. In addition, the user is not immune to their own sound attacks. Aside from that, the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses apply. Usage As shown by Eldoraggo, he mainly shouts at his targets to use the sound blasts to destroy them. Before Eldoraggo fires a sound blast, he emits a yellow aura. The aura's intensity changes depending on the power of the blast. Danny refers to his use of his ability as the Devil's Technique ( 悪魔の技 , Akuma no Waza ) .

His fighting style is similar to that of Bartholomew Kuma's, standing like a sumo wrestler and striking with open-palm thrusts which are enhanced through the use of Haki. He is also faster than his size and build would suggest, being able

ability_name: Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
ability_description: For other models of this fruit, see Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Giraffe . The Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison is a Zoan -type Devil Fruit that allows its user to transform into a full bison or a half-bison hybrid at will. It was eaten by Dalton . Etymology Ushi is Japanese for "cow" or "ox", as bisons are part of the same subfamily as cows. While the kanji is written as 野牛 ( yagyū , Japanese for "bison") , the pronunciation remains as baison , the Japanese way to pronounce the species' name. In the 4Kids dub, this fruit is simply called the Ox-Ox Fruit . In the Viz Manga, this fruit is called the Ox-Ox Fruit, Bison Model . In the Funimation dub, this fruit is called the Ox-Ox Fruit, Bison Form . In the Funimation sub, this fruit is called the Ox-Ox Fruit, Model Bison . Appearance The Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison looks like a strawberry that resembles a black bison head with white horns. It's covered in swirls except for the horn-like protrusions. It also has a green T-shaped peduncle protruding out of the calyx on top. Transformations Gallery Dalton in Beast Form. Dalton in Human-Beast Form. Strengths and Weaknesses The major strength this fruit grants, as demonstrated by Dalton , is that the user is granted two extra forms to transform into: a full bison form and a human-bison hybrid. This gives him enhanced physical capabilities, similar to a real bison's or even more. Using this as an advantage, Dalton possesses incredible strength and speed, to the point of creating afterimages. This fruit has no known weaknesses, aside from the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage This fruit's added physical capabilities allows the user to become much more deadly when fighting. Dalton uses the fruit's powers primarily for combat purposes. While it does provide increased strength, Dalton's usage of the powers are more oriented to the increased speed, dodging bullets and creating afterimages to swiftly strike down multiple targets. Techniques Fiddle Banff ( フィドル 突撃 , Fidoru Banfu , literally meaning "Fiddle Assault") : Dalton transforms into his bison-human hybrid form and then charges at his opponent with incredible speed, slashing them with his peculiarly-shaped spade. He can take out multiple opponents with this technique. This was first used on some of Wapol 's soldiers. The name Banff refers to the Banff National Park , which was once the home to Plains bisons, until they were hunted to extinction. In the Viz Manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Fiddle Bam . Translation and Dub Issues In the 4Kids dub of the anime, the fruit's name was changed to Ox-Ox Fruit in order to cater to an English-speaking audience. While this is technically correct, due to the lack of any words suggesting otherwise, the name in the 4Kids dub technically implies that Dalton turns into an ox instead of a bison. While the words "ox" and "bison" can sometimes be interchangeable, they usually refer to two different breeds of bovine. The Viz Manga and Funimation dub avoid confusion by calling it the Ox-Ox Fruit, Bison Model and Ox-Ox Fruit, Bison Form , respectively.
