Hovering the symbol may give further details. : other relevant information. Hovering the symbol gives further details. Usage This type of Haki allows its user to augment their body via their own spiritual energy, greatly increasing their power and resilience. Because of this, this form of Haki is the most used in combat. This Haki is often referred to as an "invisible armor". Basic Techniques There is a degree of ambiguity around the nature of basic Armament Haki usage. Hardening was not shown until after the timeskip, and so prior to that, the coating of users' body parts and weapons was completely invisible. Since the timeskip, however, nearly every usage of Armament Haki has explicitly involved visible Hardening, with most of the ambiguous cases being with characters who wear dark clothing or use darkened weapons which make it difficult to tell in the black-and-white manga. Even characters who used invisible Armament Haki before the timeskip have been shown using visible Hardening in the two years following. As of now, it is still unknown whether there are any functional differences between Armament Haki usages before and after the timeskip beyond the difference in appearance. In general, despite looking different and occasionally having a new degree of activation, the basic techniques of Armament Haki work very much the same way after the timeskip as they did before. Hardening Luffy using Armament Haki to harden his arms. Pica using Armament Haki to harden his entire body. Luffy's Armament Haki forming a flame-like pattern. Hardening ( 硬化 , Kōka ) is the most basic application of Armament Haki. The user clads their desired body part with a black coating which can also have shadings of a different color (particularly in the manga); the color varies among users, with Monkey D. Luffy being shown to have a crimson tint (especially while using Gear 4 ) Charlotte Katakuri having a light blue one, and Douglas Bullet having a dark blue one. With hardening, the affected body part becomes stronger in terms of both offense and defense. Hardening increases one's attack power by a considerable amount, allowing the user's blows to become tremendously destructive and dangerous. Defensively, hardening serves as an armor which can safeguard the user from a great amount of attacks, particularly ones which are not clad in Haki themselves. For example, Luffy was able to intercept Rebecca 's blunt sword with a Haki-covered forehead and shatter it. Users are capable of hardening as much of their body as they wish until they are fully coated; however, more extensive coating will lead to the Haki being depleted faster. Particularly strong concentrations of Armament Haki in a body part will cause the edges of the coating to take the shape of flames. Luffy, Big Mom , and Katakuri have been shown with flame-shaped Hardening; the necessary process to attain this is currently unknown. Notably, while this shape has only been shown appearing for Luffy and Katakuri when they are performing their strongest attacks, Big Mom generated a flame-shaped coating while casually blocking an attack from Luffy. Although hardening is extremely tough, it is not impenetrable, as strong attacks using weaker Armament Haki or even none at all have proven to be able to overwhelm hardening defenses. Typically, though, if two people with different levels of Armament Haki clash using Haki, the one with the greater degree of Armament Haki will break through the other's defenses and hurt them. Imbuement Zoro imbuing his swords with Armament Haki. Doflamingo imbuing his clothing with Armament Haki. Katakuri's mochi fists imbued with Armament Haki despite being separated from his body. Armament Haki users can also imbue their hardening onto any object they touch, thus allowing them to give their weapons the same boosts as they do their own body. Imbued weapons gain a new degree of offensive power, with one example being the Kuja warriors' imbued arrows being able to blast through solid stone. Defensively, imbuing Haki into a weapon prevents it from being damaged as easily and increases its lifespan. Dracule Mihawk , the strongest swordsman in the world, strongly believes in the importance of constantly imbuing one's sword with Armament Haki in order to prevent the blade from chipping. If a projectile weapon is imbued with Haki, the projectile will remain hardened even after it is launched away from the user's body. This has been shown with arrows, bullets, and even body parts detached via Devil Fruit powers. Additionally, Devil Fruit powers that are able to maintain a physical connection with detached body parts have displayed the ability to still be able to imbue their Armament Haki onto said detached body part. Flying slashes from imbued blades also appear to retain Haki traits and can harm Logia users. Uniquely, the Great Grade Famous Blade , Enma is capable of drawing out its wielder's Armament Haki without consent, forcing hardening. This, however, poses a great challenge to its wielder, who must "tame" it to keep their Haki from being excessively drained, to keep it from either weakening or killing them. Advanced Techniques Emission Sufficiently skilled users of Armament Haki are capable of letting the power flow out from a part of their body (usually their hand) and emitting it over a short distance without a medium to carry it. This allows the user to unleash a small invisible burst which will knock back anything in the aforementioned body part's immediate vicinity with the force of a very powerful punch. Thus, the user becomes capable of striking and harming people and objects without making physical contact with them. Given Luffy's comments about it in Wano Country , emitting Armament Haki appears to be more powerful than simply using Hardening, which is why he worked to develop this technique after his basic hardened attacks did nothing to Kaidou earlier. On Rusukaina, Silvers Rayleigh was shown being able to repel the leg of a stomping elephant and knock the creature to the ground. This ability would be a contributing factor to Luffy's victory over Kaidou; when the Emperor created a fiery version of his beast form during their final battle to prevent Luffy from touching him, Luffy was able to counter this by using emission in his final technique to defeat him. So far, Sentomaru , Boa Marigold , Kuzan , Sakazuki , Borsalino , Silvers Rayleigh , Hyougoro , Kawamatsu , Monkey D. Luffy , Gol D. Roger , Edward Newgate , Kouzuki Oden , Kaidou , Big Mom , Monkey D. Garp , Koby , and Doll have been seen emitting their Haki. Internal Destruction Grades of advanced Armament Haki (from right to left: emission and internal destruction). Luffy practicing emission and internal destruction on a tree. Luffy destroys Yamato's handcuffs. A step above the technique of emitting Armament Haki is having it flow into a targets body and destroy it from the inside out, which can be done by touching, grabbing, or even emitting Haki toward the object. This is known as Internal Destruction ( 内部破壊 , Naibu Hakai ) . Against opponents, this technique wreaks tremendous internal damage and is currently the most destructive known Armament Haki technique. This technique is also useful against objects which cannot be broken easily, as in three separate instances Rayleigh and Luffy were shown using this ability to rip off exploding collars and cuffs fast enough to throw them aside before they detonated. Luffy was later shown destroying a tree from the inside using emission, in which he caused part of the tree to burst into little pieces just by holding his fist out at it. Notably, the part of the tree that burst apart was on the opposite side from where Luffy was standing. So far only Rayleigh and Luffy have explicitly demonstrated internal destruction capabilities. Interactions with Devil Fruits Armament Haki is capable of bypassing bodily defenses granted by Devil Fruits , such as Logia intangibility or Paramecia bodily modification, allowing the Haki user to land a blow on a Devil Fruit user's "substantial body" ( 実体 , jittai ) beneath the Devil Fruit defenses. However, unlike Seastone , Armament Haki does not actually nullify the Devil Fruit power. Instead, it "forces the Devil Fruit user's body to solidify". Notably, using attacks imbued with Armament Haki on an element controlled by a Logia-type Devil Fruit user will allow the attack to bypass the intangibility and harm the "substantial body" underneath, since the element is essentially an extension of the Devil Fruit users body. However, Armament Haki attacks will not harm the "substantial body" when used on elements controlled or produced by Paramecia-type Devil Fruit users, since the element is not actually part of their body. For example, Luffy was unable to harm Trebol , even when using his Armament Haki, as what Luffy hit was a suit of armor made from the substance created by Trebol's Devil Fruit powers, and not a part of his body itself. However, this bypassing seemingly does not apply to "solid" Devil Fruit-based defenses, such as the enhanced durability, and in some cases additional natural armor, provided by Zoan transformations. Furthermore, it's indicated that strong enough Armament Haki can block other Armament's bypassing of "soft" Devil Fruit defenses, allowing abilities such as Luffy's rubber body to work as they normally would when so-armored. Devil Fruit users are capable of using Haki themselves. In fact, Devil Fruit users can use the two abilities together to make one or the other more effective. Armament Haki is capable of being imbued with substances produced by a devil fruit user (like Kizaru 's laser beams or Marco 's phoenix flames ) even if the substance is not in direct contact, as evidenced by Charlotte Katakuri, although this could also be a result of him awakening his devil fruit powers. Haki's capability to counter Devil Fruit users extends past Logia types and lets them defend against certain Paramecia effects. However, this is dependent on the strength of the Haki, as Law bypassed Vergo's Haki and bifurcated him easily by using his Devil Fruit technique, although it is unknown if Law used his own Haki to overcome Vergo's Haki. However, while Armament Haki normally asserts itself over Devil Fruit powers, the reverse is also true, with Devil Fruit users being able to empower their abilities (such as through Awakening) to levels allowing them to overwhelm the Haki of those that are otherwise significantly stronger than them. This, in turn, leaves the victim vulnerable to their powers, with several instances showing that even Supreme King Haki and Armament Haki from Emperors were unable to protect them from or fully bypass the Awakened abilities of extremely powerful Devil Fruit users. Drawbacks When two opponents using Armament Haki clash, the one with the stronger Armament Haki is more likely to be victorious. This can make it difficult to overcome an opponent's Haki in a brute force battle. However, superior Armament Haki is not an absolute defense, and can be overcome as long as the other opponent has superior physical strength. Luffy was able to damage Katakuri with Gear 4 , despite the latter noting that his own Armament Haki was superior, and this idea is shown again when Queen caught Sanji in his Brachio Coil, while boasting of how he's crushed countless Armament masters with that move. Significant physical force can also overcome this defense even if it comes from someone that does not know how to use Armament Haki; for example, prior to learning Armament Haki, Luffy struck Boa Marigold with Gear 2 techniques hard enough to send her reeling . Despite the power boost it offers, Armament Haki is not a substitute for natural physical might and it is important for Haki users to become stronger independently of Haki; Monkey D. Garp made this a point in his decades-long "Battleship Bag" training where he worked to increase his punching power without using Haki. Armament Haki is subjected to depletion if used for long periods of time as it is said to be proportional to the amount of spiritual life force an individual possesses as well as their remaining stamina. After the threshold is reached, the user will be unable to use it for a certain amount of time. It appears that the more Haki is used, the faster it will deplete. The users are unable to use Haki until they regain their stamina to use it again. The most notable example of this is Luffy's Gear 4 utilizing all of his body's Armament Haki, rendering him unable to use it again until he rests and recovers his energy. While Armament Haki is extremely good at defending against blunt strikes, its effectiveness may be diminished against piercing attacks, although this does depend on the skill of the user. Training Like the other two types of Haki, Armament Haki can occasionally be awakened without training in tense situations, such as when Portgas D. Ace manifested the ability in the heat of his battle with Draw . Unlike Observation, in which first-awakened users can be constantly plagued by increased senses, usage of Armament Haki in the heat of the moment is very temporary and the user must learn to awaken it consciously to continue using it. Also, similarly to the other Haki types, the best way to train in and develop Armament Haki is in combat, especially by battling with strong opponents. When Luffy trained to emit Armament Haki from his body, he was instructed to focus on letting the Haki "flow" out of him. Rapid progression of technique during dangerous moments can also occur here, as even before Luffy had managed to consciously begin emitting Haki, his instinct caused him to briefly awaken the ability to destroy objects internally when he and Hyougoro were on the cusp of being killed by death collars. Afterwards, Luffy was shown strengthening and refining his Haki by attacking objects, including a large block of steel, until he could damage them with ease.

The Homi Homi no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to make objects they hold fly to specific spot, making the user a Homing Human ( ホーミング人間 , Hōmingu Ningen ) . It was eaten by Homming . Etymology "Homi" is derived from "homing". Strengths and Weaknesses The fruit's main strength that it allows the user create homing projectiles by holding objects in their hands and then sending them towards a predetermined location. No weaknesses are known aside from the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Homming uses this ability to make weapons home in on his enemies.

has stated that it had been years since someone laid a hand on him before Luffy managed to do so. Katakuri's Haki can only be nullified if he stops being calm and collected, or if he faces an opponent whose level of Observation Haki rivals or surpasses his own.

The Buki Buki no Mi is a Paramecia type Devil Fruit that gives the user the ability to change their body parts into any weapon, which lets the user become a Full-Body Weapon Human ( 全身武器人間 , Zenshin Buki Ningen , English: "Full-Body Weapon") . It was eaten by Baby 5 . Etymology Buki ( 武器 ) means "weapon" in Japanese. In the VIZ manga and FUNimation adaptations, it is called the Arms-Arms Fruit , "arms" coming from "armaments". Strengths and Weaknesses The Buki Buki no Mi gives the user the ability to change their body into any kind of weapon. The user can either transform small parts of their body, like the fingers, hands or arms; or a larger body-section, such as their entire torso or even their whole body, into a weapon. Due to the plethora of weapons the user can access via this power, it would become unnecessary for them to carry or utilize external weapons. While transformed into a weapon, the user does not take any damage inflicted as a result of the weapon's impact, such as detonating while in the state of an explosive . If the user's body is broken apart, the pieces can easily reform into the user's full body, undamaged, akin to a Logia . However, this Logia-like regeneration does not apply to the user's human form, even if the user is partially transformed. Although transforming into self-detonating weapons, such as missiles and bombs, does not harm the user, the user remains vulnerable to the generated weapons if they are aimed at oneself, voluntarily or otherwise. Other than that, this fruit is weak to the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Befitting her occupation as the crew's assassin , Baby 5 uses this fruit's power for assassination and combat at both long and close range. She can transform both small individual parts of her body like transforming her finger into a gun, and she can also turn half of her body into a sword or even transmute her entire body into a rocket or explosive. For the most part, however, she usually transforms her arms, and in lesser occasion legs, into either various types of blades or firearms to complement hand-to-hand combat. Partnered with Buffalo and his rotational ability , Baby 5's weapons can gain an increase in momentum and damage range. Possessing this power grants Baby 5 the advantage of not needing to carry external weapons. Nevertheless, she does so due to her personality flaw, having bought extra weapons from a salesman who sweet talked her into it, something Buffalo reprimanded her for. Techniques Buki-morphose ( 武器変貌 , Bukimorufōze , literally meaning "Weapon Metamorphose") : Baby 5 transforms part of her body into the weapon that she wants to be. In the VIZ Manga and Funimation adaptations starting at Episode 683 , this is called Arms-morphosis . In the earlier episodes of the Funimation adaptations, this is called Weapon-morphosis . Espada Girl ( 剣女 , Esupāda Gāru , literally meaning "Sword Girl") : Baby 5 turns into a large sword, while her legs serve as the handle. It is strong enough to cut Franky 's General Shield in conjunction with Buffalo's rotational abilities. Espada is the Spanish and Portuguese word for "sword". Missile Girl ( ミサイル 女 , Misairu Gāru ) : Baby 5 turns into a large missile; Buffalo then uses his "Guru Guru Toshaho" to launch her; this only provides her the additional momentum, as she can move on her own. This combination was first used against Franky. It caused a huge explosion that toppled General Franky but otherwise caused no damage to it. After impact, Baby 5 is blown to pieces and she reforms from those fragments unharmed. Baby 5 called the effect of this attack "flaming passion". Revolver Girl ( 拳銃女 , Reboruberu Gāru , literally meaning "Pistol Girl") : Baby 5 turns into a giant gun barrel and fires rapidly. This was first used against General Franky but dealt no damage. Sickle Girl ( 鎌女 , Shikkuru Gāru ) : Baby 5 transforms herself into a kusarigama -like weapon, with her arms combining together to serve as the chain and the legs together into a massive sickle, allowing her to be spun around and used by Buffalo. It was first used against Franky, where their combined attack was strong enough to pierce General Franky's armor. The following events are non-canon , and therefore not considered part of the canon story. Flying Disk Girl ( フライング・ディスク・ガール , Furaingu Disuku Gāru ) : An anime only technique, Baby 5 transforms into a spinning top and allows herself to be thrown at the enemy by Buffalo at high speed. The attack was easily deflected by General Franky's Franken. Fire Girl ( ファイア・ガール , Faia Gāru ) : An anime only technique, Baby 5 transforms her upper body into a flamethrower . The effectiveness of the attack can be increased by Buffalo creating a gust to fan the flames, increasing their spread and ferocity, but it was still not enough to harm General Franky. Concludes non-canon section. Gatling Girl ( ガトリング・ガール , Gatoringu Gāru ) : Baby 5 transforms her right leg into a Gatling gun and fires it rapidly. It was first used on a subordinate for laughing at Pica's high-pitched voice. This technique has yet to be shown or named in the manga, but was introduced on the cover of Volume 70 . Revolver Leg ( 拳銃脚 , Reboruberu Reggu ) : Baby 5 transforms her leg into a firearm, shooting her targets at the same time as a kick. It was first used on a subordinate for laughing at Pica 's high-pitched voice, and named when used against Dagama . Other Weapons Besides from them, Baby 5 transformed some of her body parts to different weapons but she did not give them a name. Unlike "Buki-morphose", these transformations are limited to small parts of her body. Left hand as a handheld cannon . Both hands as sickles . Right hand as an axe . Left index finger as a pistol barrel . Left hand as a cleaver . Left (both in anime) hand and forearm as a musket . Right leg as a sword . Both hands as sai . Left hand as a handheld cannon . Left index finger as a pistol barrel . Left hand as a cleaver . Left (both in anime) hand and forearm as a musket . Right leg as a sword . Both hands as sai . History At least 13 years ago, Baby 5 ate the Buki Buki no Mi, as she used the ability against Wellington when she was 11.

His eyes take on an appearance similar to those of Sea Kings. In this state, Hody seems to have gone mad to which Shirahoshi commented that he became far more terrifying than he

ability_name: Batto Batto no Mi, Model: Vampire
ability_description: For the other known model of this fruit, see Batto Batto no Mi . The Batto Batto no Mi, Model: Vampire is a game-exclusive , Mythical Zoan -type Devil Fruit that gives the user the ability to turn into a vampire. It was eaten by Patrick Redfield . Etymology Batto is the Japanese pronunciation for the word "bat". In the English subs, it is called the Bat-Bat Fruit: Model Vampire . Appearance The Batto Batto no Mi is a purple tomato-shaped fruit with swirl patterns around it and a curly green stem resembling a tail. The bottom of the fruit is dark-red resembling a pool of blood, a trait that's associated with Vampires. The fruit is small enough to be held easily in a man's hand. Strengths and Weaknesses The main strength of the fruit's power is that it allows the user to transform into a full vampire or assume a vampiric hybrid form. This gives the user the abilities of flight, to create a shroud to cloak themselves, and an apparent form of teleportation by appearing and disappearing in blood-red bursts of energy. The greatest strength of this fruit is the ability to suck someone else's life-force either by biting the victim's neck or digging their nails into the victim's skin. By sucking the victim's life-force, the user can age them to regain their own youth, with Red implying the fruit grants immortality and eternal youth as long as there is life-force for the user to drain. The user can also return the life-force to to their victims if they choose to do so, and there were no ill effects to the victims after their life-force was returned. It is unknown if the fruit gives the user the usual weaknesses of a vampire such as sunlight, garlic or crosses, but the user does require taking another person's life-force to use the fruit's power to its full potential, as when Red gave the life-force he stole back to his victims, he stopped using it (except for the ability to create shrouds). Also, an opponent can still see the user in the shroud they create by focusing on their glowing eyes and claws. Otherwise, it has standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Redfield obtained and ate this fruit as a means of regaining his youth. He then sucks out Yadoya and Luffy 's life-force, leaving the latter in a state similar to aging him by many years. Luffy is left with white hair and gray skin, and his aged body made it difficult for him to fight. He uses his wings and claws to swipe and or cut his opponent. History Past Sometime after Patrick was released from Impel Down by Marshall D. Teach , he gained the Batto Batto no Mi, Model: Vampire and ate it.

nature, to the point that Ace’s very presence was able to stop blizzards on an island for as long as he was there. As such, when he turned up on Drum Island, no snow fell at all on the island for an entire day. Supreme King Haki Ace possessed Supreme King Haki, which he initially had no conscious control over. He first demonstrated the ability at 10 years of age

when he rendered the Bluejam Pirates except for Bluejam unconscious after an outburst of rage. He later gained knowledge of the power, as he recognized it when Luffy used it at the Summit War of Marineford. He was never seen using this Haki apart from in his childhood, save for in video games (such as during the activation of his Kizuna Rush in Pirate Warriors 3 ) and One Piece

War of Marineford, he managed to punch and hurt Marco, despite the latter being in his regenerative phoenix form. He can also utilize the advanced application of emitting Armament Haki out from his hands, allowing him to attack opponents without making physical contact. Observation Haki Garp is an extremely skilled and experienced user of Observation Haki. He was able to sense and catch Shiryu's attack when he attempted to attack

So far, the characters who have clearly shown the use of Haki or have been stated to be able to use it are: , those hit with Supreme King Haki foam at the mouth after or while losing consciousness, whereas, in the In the anime, the use of Supreme King and Armament Haki creates a rippling or shockwave-like effect, similar to the , while in the manga, it is completely invisible. Also, in recent episodes, the use of Supreme King Haki causes the affected area, excluding the user, to gain a dark blue tint. However, this could just be for dramatic effect. In the

Black Leg Style Diable Jambe Ifrit Jambe Team Combinations Anime Only Here listed are the Black Leg Style techniques that are only found in the anime or movies. List of Non-Canon Techniques Black Leg Style Tekka Shoot ( テッカシュート , Tekka Shūto , literally meaning "Iron-Fire Shoot") : Sanji catches the ball on his leg, and then spins around upside-down like in Party Table Kick Course, sending the ball flying out of the park. Collier Turret ( コリエトーッレト , Korie Tōrreto , literally meaning "Neck Tower") : Sanji stands up on Usopp 's back in order to avoid slipping (as they were in a frozen cavern), resulting in them resembling a turret, and then delivers a powerful kick to the opponent's neck. It was used against Salchow of the Accino Family . Armée de L'Air Robo Shoot ( 空軍 ロボシュート , Arume do Rēru Robo Shūto , literally meaning "Air Force Robo Shoot") : Sanji launches Franky , Usopp, and Chopper while using Pirates Docking 3. This move was used against Oars . Gomu Gomu no Kaiten Ono ( ゴムゴムの回転斧 , Gomu Gomu no Kaiten Ono , literally meaning "Rubber Rubber Spinning Axe") : A combination of moves executed by Luffy , Zoro , Sanji, and Usopp. Luffy executes Gomu Gomu no Fusen as well as twisting his body, using the stored air to launch himself into the air. Then, Usopp aboard the Merry-Go fires a cannonball at Sanji, while Luffy shoots both of his arms towards the target. Sanji at this point Armeé de l'Air Hana Shoots the cannonball at Zoro who executes 108 Pound Cannon to fire the cannonball up into the air and toward Luffy. Luffy catches the ball with his feet, the inertia of the cannonball spinning him vertically several times causing his legs to twist with each revolution. After all of the cannonball's inertia is gone, Luffy drops it and proceeds to spin forward releasing the full force of the stored energy from the winding, his body falling towards the target during all this. As Luffy approaches the target he stretches his legs out, which, through the speed of his spinning, creates the image of a large rotary blade. This attack impacts with the target, cutting it in half. The force of this seems out of Luffy's control since Zoro and Sanji has to stop him after cutting his target. This was first used to destroy Ratchet 's castle. Sexy Fire ( セクシーファイヤー , Sekushī Faiyā ) : A variation of Hell Memories. Using memories of Nami's sexiness, Sanji sets his entire body on fire in preparation for an attack. Koi no Meteor Strike ( 恋のメテオストライク , Koi no Meteo Sutoraiku , literally meaning "Meteor Strike of Love") : After igniting his body with Sexy Fire, he flies towards the enemy like a meteor and delivers a strong kick, slamming the opponent to the ground. It was first seen being used to defeat Binz . This is called Meteor Strike of Love in the Funimation adaptations. Remember My Dreams ( リーメンバー マイ ドリームス , Rīmenbā Mai Dorīmusu ) : In a similar manner to Hell Memories, Sanji set himself on fire and charges forward, using Sky Walk to accelerate, before kicking the opponent. It was used to defeat Psycho P . Hell Memories Gomu Shot ( 地獄の思い出 ゴムショット , Heru Memorīzu Gomu Shotto , literally meaning "Memories of Hell Rubber Shoot") : Sanji activates Hell Memories before kicking Luffy, causing Luffy to be engulfed in flames after being launched. This is called Hell Memories Gum Shoot in the Funimation adaptations. Ground Walk ( 地歩行 , Guraundo Wōku , literally meaning "Earth Walk") : Sanji kicks off the the ground to dash towards his opponent at high speed in order to launch a follow up attack. Ryusei Osoba Kick ( 流星 おそばキック , Ryūsei Osoba Kick , literally meaning "Meteor Soba Kick") : While in midair, Sanji descends at extreme speed and delivers a powerful kick with the heel accelerator of his Raid Suit . It was used during his fight as O-Soba Mask against Page One . Despite being powerful enough to create a crater in the Flower Capital , it was not enough to knock out the Shinuchi . This is called Shooting Star Soba Kick in the Funimation adaptations. Diable Jambe Venaison Shoot ( 野獣肉 シュート , Vunezon Shūto , literally meaning "Venison Shoot") : Sanji spinds horizontally in the air and kicks the opponent repeatedly in the chest and belly before finishing the attack with a powerful axe kick to the head with added heat and strength of Diable Jambe. This attack was first seen being used to defeat Scarlet . Amour Shot ( アムルショット , Amūru Shotto , Literally meaning:"Love Shot") : A powerful straight kick aimed just below the diaphragm that sends an opponent flying. This technique was used to finish of a minion of the Donquixote Pirates , who Sanji was led to believe had caused Violet to cry.

Gaban has also mentioned his belief that they are not truly immortal as shown by Loki, who is able to kill Harald when his mark of was severed and his sense came back temporarily. Foxfire Style is a style of sword fighting used by the samurai , Kin'emon . Later on, Roronoa Zoro learned how to perform the style and implement it in his swordsmanship. Overview Foxfire Style allows the user to generate, attack with, and cut through fire. This ability allows Kin'emon to cut through flames and explosions while completely protecting himself from harm, giving it a strong defensive use. For offense, the generated fire enhances Kin'emon's regular attacks and is useful against creatures susceptible to heat. This style can further be used for first aid, with the fire being capable of cauterizing potentially deadly wounds. Roronoa Zoro , having seen Kin'emon use this fighting style, later uses it to cut Prometheus in half, and then started combining it with his own techniques. Techniques Kin'emon Homura Saki ( 焔裂き , Homura Saki , literally meaning "Flame Split") : Kin'emon slashes his sword to slice an incoming fire attack. This was first used to cut through an explosion caused by pieces of Smiley and was first named when slicing through the flame breath of one of Vegapunk 's dragons. This attack was used again against Kaidou , slicing through his Bolo Breath and wounding him at the same time. In the VIZ manga, this is initially called Blaze Slice . In the Funimation adaptations, this is initially called Flame Split . Later, the english adaptations call it Flame-Rend . Karyu Issen ( 火柳一閃 , Karyū Issen , literally meaning "Firework Flash") : Kin'emon leaps into the air to attack with a powerful flaming slash. This was first used to defeat one of Vegapunk's dragons. This technique is similar to Zoro 's Hiryu: Kaen technique. This is called Fire-Willow Flash in the VIZ manga and Flaming Flash in the Funimation adaptations. Roronoa Zoro Homura Saki ( 焔裂き , Homura Saki , literally meaning "Flame Split") : One of Kin'emon's techniques performed by Zoro, which was able to split the sun homie Prometheus in half. Homura Saki: Rokudo no Tsuji ( 焔裂き 六道の辻 , Homura Saki: Rokudō no Tsuji , literally meaning "Flame Split: Crossing of the Six Paths") : Using Three Sword Style's Rokudo no Tsuji and Homura Saki together, Zoro continuously cuts flames into several small pieces within seconds. It was first used against Prometheus, rendering him weakened for a considerable time by increasing the effort it needed to reform. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Flame-Rend: Crossing the Six Paths .

Haki As a Vice Admiral of the Marines, Garp is fully capable of using Haki. and is one of the very few people in the world who can use all three types of Haki. He is also a capable teacher, as he taught Kuzan, Koby and Helmeppo. Garp's Haki is potent enough to protect him from the deep-freezing effects of Kuzan's devil fruit abilities before easily breaking out of it.

"Devil's Fruit" redirects here. For information about Devil Fruits in general, see Devil Fruit . The Akuma no Mi is a Devil Fruit that allows its user to transform into a devil at will and access numerous demonic abilities which they can bestow upon others in exchange for special control over their bodies. The fruit was eaten by Nerona Imu . Etymology Akuma ( 悪魔 ) is the Japanese word for "Devil" or "Demon". This ability's name is unique among Devil Fruits, as it is a single three-syllable word rather than a two-syllable pattern repeated once. The name is homophonous with the Japanese term for Devil Fruits in general; the only difference is that Akuma ( アクマ ) is spelled using katakana instead of kanji. To distinguish this fruit's name from Devil Fruits in general, the VIZ translation has rendered it as " The Devil's Fruit ". It is the only officially translated fruit name to utilize the possessive (which the no ( の ) particle in the Japanese name is for). Transformation Imu's silhouetted transformation. Imu's transformation whilst possessing Gunko. Imu's devil transformation appears to resemble common depictions of Satan . While his complete transformation is still silhouetted, he is still extremely large, being roughly equivalent to the Five Elders' massive Mythical Zoan Beast Forms in size and occupying a considerable portion of the enormous platform beneath the Empty Throne . In the form displayed on Elbaph, Imu is several times larger than an average human, but is still much smaller than a giant . The transformation affords numerous demonic features and accessories, such as a pair of curly horns on the user's forehead and a long dark tail which bears at least three horned skulls on it. The tail appears to be tattered, or has an essence which is not entirely solid. The user also possesses a pair of large, feathered black wings that they can appear and disappear at will. Imu's transformation bears a ring of black flames looming behind his head, noticeably different from those who have awakened their ability, who are decorated with clouds that float around their neck and arms. The other main difference is this ring of flames bears 16 eyes on it which are identical in appearance to Imu's. Smaller puffs of these flames have also been shown floating around Imu a greater distance away. The pattern of the flames, along with the eyes within, are unique in that they are not seen in any other awakened Zoan users so far. When possessing Gunko's body, Imu activated a slightly different transformation, which did not increase Gunko's size. Gunko's body only gained a devil tail, wings, and a long pitchfork trident; notably, the wings looked considerably different than the ones shown on Imu himself, as they resemble bat wings. Strengths and Weaknesses Given the fruit's unique name and abilities, it is not certain if it can be classified like a normal Devil Fruit. However, the transformation and its numerous esoteric applications heavily resemble the rare and powerful Mythical Zoan class. This unusual Devil Fruit boasts an incredibly wide range of supernatural powers, and it forms not only the basis of Imu's abilities but also those of his closest subordinates, the Five Elders and Knights of God , with all of these parties being powerful combatants. With their wings, which they can individually manifest at will, the user becomes capable of flight. The devil's tail can be weaponized as a fast and powerful piercing appendage, as demonstrated when Imu used his tail to impale Sabo, a Logia user, while the latter was trying to escape with King Cobra . The tail is quite durable and can clash with powerful blades; Imu effortlessly used it to block an attack by Zoro using highly graded blades imbued with Supreme King Haki . Imu was also completely unaffected in his fully transformed state when Sabo attacked him with his fire , which he simply ate. The user can summon a levitating grimoire -like book with a pentagram on the cover seemingly out of thin air. From this book, they are able to manifest a dark-colored substance which can quickly transform into a variety of weapons, with these weapons being powerful enough to severely injure the giant Brogy . The user is seemingly capable of outputting a telekinetic force to attack from a distance. When Brook charged at Imu as he was possessing Gunko, the latter merely raised a hand, pointed in his direction and struck Brook down from afar, knocking his sword from his hand. Imu then used this ability to redirect one of Usopp 's explosive shots back at him. Later, Imu was able to dissipate a lightning blast from Loki in his Nidhöggr form by holding his index finger to his thumb on both hands (similar to a Mudra gesture ) and putting his hands out, holding the left hand's ring on the right palm. When Imu transported himself to Elbaph, a few nearby trees and wooden houses of the Western Village reacted to his appearance and transformed into demonic faces that began moving and singing as though they had become animated creatures, resembling homies created by the Soru Soru no Mi . When Imu left the Western Village, the houses went back to normal. It is unknown if the user is affected by the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses, given the unique nature of this Devil Fruit. Immortality The fruit's powers appear to play a role in the "immortal body" ( 不死の体 , fushi no karada ) that Imu possesses and has granted to his subordinates. Notably, when Imu possessed Jaygarcia Saturn on God Valley , he managed to regenerate with ease after being struck by simultaneous attacks from Rocks D. Xebec , Gol D. Roger , Monkey D. Garp , Edward Newgate , Charlotte Linlin , and Kaidou , even though many of the strikes were infused by Supreme King Haki which is effective against a Covenantee's immortality. Abyss The fruit allows the user to generate a magic circle known as an Abyss, which can enable instantaneous transportation from one location to another. By summoning an Abyss on the ground, the user can move themselves to any other location that bears an Abyss. Additionally, the user is capable of summoning smaller Abyss circles in thin air from their hands that can summon objects such as weapons, which the user can immediately make use of. When a user of this ability transports themself or another person with an Abyss, significant quantities of Haki are shown being emitted. Imu can grant other people the ability to travel around through the Abyss only if they have a covenant with Imu. Notably, while the Five Elders and Knights of God have only used the Abyss for travel, Imu can make extensive use of the Abyss in combat. Imu summoning cannon barrels through an Abyss. Imu's appearance accompanied by a massive Abyss circle in the sky. Covenant By placing special "marks" on other people's bodies, the user is capable of enabling them to access many of the fruit's aforementioned abilities; this technique is referred to by Imu as a Covenant, and it can come in different forms. In return for sharing their own powers, the fruit user gains a special level of control over the Covenantees which appears to depend on the severity of Covenant issued. If the user gives a Covenantee a direct order, they will not be able to defy it. Imu has shown the ability to communicate telepathically with certain Covenantees and possess their bodies outright; the user can do this even when the Covenantee is a significant distance away. However, the user can only use a limited amount of their own power through a possession. The user can manipulate the Covenant so as to remove it from a Covenantee and kill them; when used on Jaygarcia Saturn , the process involved a dark, flame-like substance being emitted from and around his body, leading to him withering away and resulting in a large explosion, which left only his skeleton. The user can physically manifest a duplicate of themself from an individual to possess them. When possessing Saturn's body on God Valley , Imu was able to manifest his own body from Saturn's back, rearing up above him as a massive silhouette from which his eyes and even crown took shape. While possessing Gunko on Elbaph , Imu physically emerged from Gunko's torso as an elongated black silhouette and after being frozen, appeared to simply fade away, leaving Gunko behind. This manifestation appears to have limitations in what he can do, given that in both known case Imu only used this to preform Domi Reversi on his enemy. However, making a Covenant with another person also requires a "cost" paid by the user as well. The specifics of this drawback are yet to be revealed, but after Harald was killed by Loki despite his immortality, Imu was in pain and refused to send any more Knights of God to Elbaph . There may be a limit to the number of advanced Covenants one can give, as Imu limited his Depths and Abyssal Covenants to just 13 in number overall. Another form of Covenant is capable of transforming unwilling victims into demons ; the user can seemingly inflict this transformation rather quickly and easily just by summoning an Abyss or manifesting tendrils to strike their target. Once transformed, the target possesses an immortal body and greatly enhanced strength, which Imu dubs the "power of god" ( 神の力 , kami no chikara ) . Unlike the recipients of other Covenants, demons completely lose their original personalities and free will, becoming mindless and violent and completely beholden to the user's will at every waking moment. However, these demons can be reverted back to normal when struck with lethal blows, which will also heal the lethal blow and restore them to life. Additionally, some individuals like Luffy and Loki are completely immune to becoming demons at all. Curiously, Chopper , at least in his Monster Point form , is capable of restoring demons back to normal by simply attacking without dealing fatal damage, though the exact reason is as of yet unknown. Imu believes that two of the tenets of domination play a role in these powers: corruption ( 堕落 , daraku ) , the result of a lust for power that is too easily gained, is behind demon transformation, while Covenants stem from a desire to serve someone out of self-interest. Omen For the chapter of the same name, see Chapter 1180 . Omen ( 魔気 , Ōmen , lit. "Demonic Aura") is a black flame that the user can generate from their body or weapons; Imu refers to it as his "military might" ( 軍事力 , gunji-ryoku ) and has claimed that it is power ( 魔気 , chikara ) stemming from goal-driven envy. The Omen flame possesses a vast amount of capabilities which enable it to be used for attack, defense, and support in combat. Offensively, it can unleash massive explosions and take the form of just about any weapon, with its shifting time being so fast that it can become a blade that easily impales anyone in its vicinity. Defensively, it has shown itself capable of countering a strike from the monstrously powerful giant Loki wielding Ragnir , which came imbued with Loki's lightning and Supreme King Haki . In terms of support mechanisms, the user has been shown to use Omen to make their own body grow in size, even to the point of reaching giant size. Additionally, by being placed as a ring around at least Covenantees like the Knights of God, their bodies are given a boost in strength. The black flame projectiles created through Omen seemingly transform into ordinary steel bullets and bombs after their power is expended. Imu generates the Omen flame from his hand. The Omen setting off a large explosion. An Omen ring bestowed upon a Covenantee. Imu defends using the Omen. Usage Since he is almost always inactive due to his secretive existence, Imu's fruit abilities are most commonly manifested in the subordinates he grants Covenants to, which are the Five Elders and Knights of God. Imu maintains a three-tiered system for his Covenants, with these levels being known as Shallows, Depths, and Abyssal Covenants. Shallows Covenants are the most limited, being given to Devoted Blades of God; while they receive no special powers, Imu's control over them is limited to just Mary Geoise. Once one becomes a full Knight of God, they are given the Depths Covenant, and here they become immortal and able to use the Abyss, but are subject to Imu's control anywhere in the world. In the rare instances that Imu wishes to personally intervene in an affair (something that is only known to have happened twice in the past century), he will possess a covenantee who is currently at the relevant location. Imu rarely chooses to personally engage in any form of combat, which is implied to be due to a mysterious problem that discourages him from leaving the Holy Land for long. Due to usually only appearing outside of Mary Geoise through possession of his subordinates, he instead relies on the "Demon Covenant" to turn his enemies into demonic allies who will do most of the work for him. When he does engage in physical combat, however, he has been shown to make swift work of anyone who dares to attack him. Rather than allowing for any sort of drawn out fight, he will utilize small, demonic projectiles to blow up his opponents. When forced to use his body, his attacks cause similar explosions, as well as black flames. This form of combat appears to be Imu's main go to for defensive situations as well, as he relied heavily on his transformation and abilities to subdue Nefertari Cobra and Sabo in the Empty Throne chamber. He utilized his hybrid form upon arriving to Elbaph to swiftly free his subordinates. Although this left him open to attack, Imu simply allowed his opponents to approach, used his devil tail to block a Supreme King Haki attack by Roronoa Zoro , and took advantage of his large size to easily block and counter a kick from Sanji . When approached by Loki, a giant with ancient giant heritage, Imu adapated by gigantifying his own body to nearly match his enemy in terms of size, allowing him to defend and clash evenly with Loki. Techniques A Kuwaru ( 悪魔契約 , Ā Kuwāru , lit. "Demon Covenant") : Imu creates an Abyss as a means of transforming a target into a demon . In exchange for a portion of the target's lifespan, they are granted heightened superhuman strength. It can be applied by two methods: The first is for Imu to conjure the Abyss in the air with his hand, which is then projected down beneath his target for him to sink into; the second is for Imu is to apply the Abyss to a pair of tendrils that emerge from his body to stab into a target. The name of this technique is likely a pun on the Japanese words aku and waru ( 悪 ) , which mean "evil." In the VIZ manga, this pact is called Diabolical Covenant . Domi Reversi ( 黒転支配 , Domi Ribāshi , lit. "Black Conversion Control") : After Imu's target becomes partially submerged into the Abyss and begins transforming into a demon, Imu flips his hand and rotates the target so that they become fully submerged. If two demons stand on either side of another target, then that target—having been caught between them—will also undergo the same process and transform into a demon. This is similar to the rules of the board game reversi . Imu can also initiate the transformation directly without flipping them by stabbing the target with his black tendrils. While there is no known defense to this technique, sufficient willpower can delay the transformation, even if only briefly. Interestingly, only Monkey D. Luffy and Loki have proven to be immune to the effects of Domi Reversi, the reason for which is unknown. Imu uses Domi Reversi via impalement on Rocks. Imu uses Domi Reversi via an Abyss on Brogy. Nemesis ( 天罰剣 , Nemeshisu , lit. "Divine Punishment Sword") : Imu uses Omen to shapeshift his polearm into a sword-staff, then causes an Omen flame shaped like a blade to shoot forth from the hilt, impaling his opponent. This technique was first seen in use against Loki , easily running him through. Nemesis (Ancient Greek: Νέμεσις) is the goddess of divine retribution in Greek mythology. In the VIZ manga, this is named as Blade of Judgment--Nemesis . Tzitzimitl ( 日食爆撃 , Tsitsimitoru , lit. "Solar Eclipse Bombing") : Imu enlarges the circle of Omen at his back and uses it as a medium to fire numerous Omen projectiles shaped like flaming demon heads, causing massive explosions over a wide area. This was first used against Loki. A Tzitzimitl is a skeletal female deity from Aztec mythology, later considered a type of "demon" in post-conquest descriptions; they are associated with the stars seen around the sun during a solar eclipse. In the VIZ manga, this is named as Gazing Eclipse . Gram ( 憤怒剣 , Guramu , lit. "Wrathful Sword") : Imu imbues his weapon with Omen so that two long blades of dark flame are formed at either end of his polearm, before proceeding to swing the enlarged double-bladed weapon in a circle. This move was first seen in use against Loki, to deflect the latter's lightning bolts summoned from the clouds. Gram (Old Norse: Gramr , "Wrath") is a legendary sword of Norse mythology, used by the hero Sigurd to slay the dragon Fafnir. In the VIZ manga, the move is named as Blade of Wrath--Gram .

Sufficiently skilled users of Armament Haki are capable of letting the power flow out from a part of their body (usually their hand) and emitting it over a short distance without a medium to carry it. This allows the user to unleash a small invisible burst which will knock back anything in the aforementioned body part's immediate vicinity with the force of a very powerful punch. Thus, the user becomes capable of striking and harming people and objects without making physical contact with them. , emitting Armament Haki appears to be more powerful than simply using Hardening, which is why he worked to develop this technique after his basic hardened attacks did nothing to

into the future with his Observation Haki aids his dodging ability considerably, and during his fight with Kaidou on Onigashima, Luffy managed to partially dodge Kaidou's Raimei Hakke attack despite the Emperor being capable of moving at near-blinding speed; earlier, Luffy had been powerless to avoid that strike.
