Usage This type of Haki grants the user the ability to dominate the wills of others. Unlike the other types of Haki, Supreme King Haki cannot be attained through training, instead its users must be born with the ability and can only learn to control it after discovering that they have it. Only one in several million people are born with this Haki. Portgas D. Ace was shown unwittingly using Supreme King Haki at the age of 10. Additionally, Supreme King Haki cannot be strengthened through direct use like the other two forms of Haki. Rather, the user can increase their force of will by becoming physically stronger. Thus, although the ability can manifest at a young age, Supreme King Haki users generally do not consciously manifest and make full use of their abilities until adulthood. The ability to affect someone depends on the Haki user having greater strength of will than the person the Haki is being used against. The greater the power gap, the more easily the victim will succumb. Those with stronger wills can resist or even ignore the effects of Supreme King Haki. It is unknown if there is a maximum range for Supreme King Haki, but it can at least reach several kilometers. Basic Techniques Domination Motobaro panicking from Luffy's close range Supreme King Haki. Ryokugyu intimidated by Shanks' long range Supreme King Haki. Supreme King Haki can be used to temporarily dominate the psyche of others and intimidate them. This has been most explicitly shown against animals, with usages of Supreme King Haki causing them to either become fearful and flee or become docile for a brief period, with both effects happening instantly. This domination may end up resulting in the victim losing consciousness a short time later, as shown by Motobaro . Additionally, the kraken Surume was shown obeying commands from Luffy after being intimidated by a glare from him, but the overall degree of control that Supreme King Haki users can assert over animals purely with this Haki is unclear. On Amazon Lily , when Luffy unleashed Supreme King Haki while shouting at Boa Marigold and Boa Sandersonia to stop attacking their petrified countrywomen, the two sisters were shown to be taken aback by the outburst and ultimately obeyed his command, though it is uncertain whether their action was unconsciously influenced by the Supreme King Haki usage to any degree. In other instances, people in the vicinity of Supreme King Haki usage whose wills are strong enough to avoid incapacitation have not demonstrated much indication of being dominated by the user's will. While Supreme King Haki users are almost always shown in high-ranking leadership positions, it remains unknown if this Haki plays any sort of a role in their ability to attract followers (other than people who consciously follow Supreme King Haki users out of admiration for the Haki like Doflamingo's executives). Of course, since Supreme King Haki users are stated to have the "qualities of a king", they are naturally expected to possess personality traits that make them effective leaders. However, sufficiently strong Supreme King Haki users have been shown able to exert a constant uncomfortable pressure against their targets and can use this domination to explicitly coerce them into obeying. This was demonstrated by Shanks against Admiral Ryokugyu, who appeared to be in pain and almost paralyzed by the force. In addition, Shanks seemed able to communicate with Ryokugyu somewhat through his Haki. Incapacitation Doflamingo using his Haki to knock people unconscious. Luffy, unfamiliar with the ability, knocking out weaker-willed people indiscriminately. By overpowering the will of those around them to an even greater degree, a Supreme King Haki user is capable of knocking people and animals in their vicinity unconscious. Whether or not a person is rendered unconscious depends on the degree of their strength in comparison to the user's; generally, normal citizens and common soldiers and pirates are most likely to be knocked out by Supreme King Haki, whereas people established to be capable, powerful, and strong-willed are far less likely to fall victim; Oda has stated that anyone who is knocked out by Supreme King Haki is essentially "not worth fighting" the user directly. People who just barely have enough willpower to withstand a Supreme King Haki user have been shown to become light-headed and are forced to concentrate in order to remain conscious, and although sufficiently powerful people can resist this power without issue, they are still able to sense the Haki being used. The greater the strength and willpower that a Supreme King Haki user possesses, the more people they are capable of incapacitating. Sufficiently powerful Supreme King Haki users are capable of going up against large hordes of enemies and incapacitating most, if not all of them in an instant. It is even possible to knock out tens of thousands of enemies at once, as demonstrated by Luffy, who knocked out 50,000 members of the New Fish-Man Pirates . According to Oda, Shanks or Rayleigh could have knocked out all 100,000. Unknowing or inexperienced Supreme King Haki users frequently first manifest this ability unconsciously by shouting at those around them with the intent of trying to get them to stop doing something, and their release of Supreme King Haki will tend to indiscriminately impact everyone in their vicinity who is weak-willed enough. With training, a Supreme King Haki user is capable of incapacitating people simply by looking intently at them, or even by just walking past them. Sufficiently skilled Supreme King Haki users can also limit the effect of their Haki when using it in a crowded area, being capable of choosing not to knock out certain people. Additionally, Supreme King Haki users have even demonstrated the ability to incapacitate others while unconscious, with their spirit remaining unyielding even as their body gives out. Advanced Techniques Infusion Roger and Newgate clashing with Supreme King Haki infusion. Luffy infusing his body with Supreme King Haki to dramatically increase his attack power. Garp infusing his fist with Supreme King Haki, which releases in a shock wave large enough to decimate an entire town. Gaban decapitates an immortal individual using Supreme King Haki infusion on his axe, blocking his regeneration. Roger and Garp using Supreme King Haki at "critical mass". A very small number of Supreme King Haki users, stated to only be a "handful" of the very strongest by Kaidou, are able to "infuse" ( まとえる , matoeru , lit. "(to) wear/be clad (in)") their Supreme King Haki, in a way that is similar to the Color of Armament , greatly augmenting the power of their physical attacks. After someone clads themself (or their weapon) with Supreme King Haki, the black lightning usually seen with it appears, if more prominently. In the anime, the emitted Supreme King Haki is occasionally made visible by a colored aura surrounding the user. This application of Supreme King Haki provides such an immense boost in power, it allowed Luffy to finally deal meaningful damage to Kaidou, whereas his earlier attacks boosted with advanced (emitted) Armament Haki had been "too shallow" and had no significant effect. Luffy was also able to use the "cladding" of Supreme King Haki to deflect Kaidou's kanabo with a kick whereas the best he could do with his highly trained Armament had been barely blocking a kanabo strike with both arms, and his arms were left in pain afterwards. All those who have been seen able to infuse Supreme King Haki are also extremely skilled in Armament Haki, which can also be used in conjunction with Supreme King infusion to augment the overall effectiveness of both Haki types. The advanced Armament application of damaging objects without making physical contact with them has often been seen with people using Supreme King Haki infusion, but it should not be taken as a sign of Supreme King Haki infusion by itself, as it has been shown that infused objects can make physical contact with what they strike. The infusion of Supreme King Haki is also required to harm immortal individuals. As demonstrated by Gaban, on both occasions, their ability to regenerate from regular and fatal damage was blocked for a short time. Gaban has also mentioned his belief that they are not truly immortal as shown by Loki, who is able to kill Harald when his mark of covenant was severed and his sense came back temporarily. Aside from offense, Supreme King Haki "infusion" could also be used for defense, as shown by Garp and Xebec. Garp was able to withstand a combined attack of Zeus and Prometheus unscathed. Xebec protected himself from both the attacks of Roger and Garp, despite them using Armament Haki boosted with Supreme King Haki infusion, thus cancelling their attacks. Its defense was so immensely powerful that the pair needed to risk using the "critical mass" of their Supreme King Haki to finally damage the demonized Xebec. So far, the only individuals seen capable of infusing Supreme King Haki are Gol D. Roger , Edward Newgate , Kaidou , Monkey D. Luffy , Charlotte Linlin , Yamato , Roronoa Zoro , Silvers Rayleigh , Shanks , Monkey D. Garp , Scopper Gaban , Rocks D. Xebec , and Loki . Tying It is possible to "tie" ( 結ぶ , musubu ) a concentration of Supreme King Haki into a knot, storing it for seemingly an indefinite amount of time. When the knot is undone, the Supreme King Haki is unleashed in a burst much like the standard use of Supreme King Haki, incapacitating those with weaker wills. Currently, the only person known to have used this technique is Joy Boy , who tied his Supreme King Haki into a knot using rope, and locked it inside Emet where it remained for around 800 years after Joy Boy's death. It is unknown whether any particular rope or material is needed to accomplish this. Observation Killing Observation Killing ( 見聞殺し , Kenbun-Goroshi ) is an advanced Supreme King Haki technique which allows for the control of one's own aura or presence, negating an opponent's ability to see into the future with Observation Haki . This technique has only been briefly mentioned so far, with Shanks being the only confirmed user. The Qualities of a King According to Marigold and Trebol , Supreme King Haki is proof of an individual who possesses the "qualities of a king " ( 王の資質 , ō no shishitsu ) , a candidate chosen by heaven. This can outright manifest in an ambition to become a "king" of some kind, based on the statements of Chinjao and King . However, Supreme King Haki users need not been born of a royal bloodline, nor does it require one to strive to become royalty in the literal sense; it only needs one to have the greatest of ambitions (the goal of earning a significant position, the strive to be the best in the world at something, etc.). Many of the people who have been shown to possess Supreme King Haki share a royal position or epithet or attained some highly respected status, which indicates their dominating role: Gol D. Roger was the captain of the Roger Pirates , the only crew in history to circumnavigate the Grand Line , attaining the title of "Pirate King" for his unparalleled accomplishments among pirates. Silvers Rayleigh was the right-hand man of the Pirate King Roger and is known by his epithet "Dark King". Shanks is the chief of the Red Hair Pirates , a former apprentice to Gol D. Roger and one of the Four Emperors who ruled over territory in the New World. He spoke to Edward Newgate as an equal, and is currently on track to claiming the One Piece . In addition, he is one of the two biological sons of the former commander of the Knights of God and one of the current Five Elders , Saint Figarland Garling . Monkey D. Luffy is the son of Monkey D. Dragon, the grandson of Monkey D. Garp and the sworn brother of Portgas D. Ace and Sabo. He is also the captain of the Straw Hat Pirates , among the fastest-ascending pirate leaders in the present day, showing the drive to become the next Pirate King. His ability to attract followers is well-renowned, particularly after over 5000 pirates swore allegiance to him and formed the Straw Hat Grand Fleet despite Luffy not desiring this, and these events have caused him to be acknowledged as one of new members of the Four Emperors. Portgas D. Ace was the son of Gol D. Roger, the adoptive grandson of Monkey D. Garp, and was the sworn brother of Monkey D. Luffy and Sabo. He was also the captain of the Spade Pirates , having gained enough notoriety to get invited into the Seven Warlords of the Sea at one point. He would later join the Whitebeard Pirates, becoming the 2nd Division Commander, similar to Kouzuki Oden. Edward Newgate was the captain of the Whitebeard Pirates and formerly was one of the Four Emperors who ruled over territory in the New World. He was widely considered the closest rival to the Pirate King Roger, possessing the titles of "Strongest Man in the World" and "Man Closest to One Piece" until his death. Monkey D. Garp is known as the "Hero of the Marines". He always refuses a promotion to the rank of Admiral due to not wanting to be subservient to the World Nobles , and constantly does whatever he wishes despite orders. Garp has clashed with several powerful pirates in his time, including the Pirate King Gol D. Roger and several other conquerors, and even continues to be active after his prime. He was also someone close to the Pirate King, having fought alongside him before he attained this title, and before Roger's execution spoke to him as a confidant. Sengoku was the fleet admiral of the Marines until two years ago and was one of the strongest and most renowned Marines during the era of Roger. In addition, both his epithet ("the Buddha") and Devil Fruit are in reference to the founder of a religion . Charlotte Linlin , better known as "Big Mom", is the captain of the Big Mom Pirates , formerly one of the Four Emperors, and the official queen of the Totto Land archipelago. She allied with Kaidou with the goal of conquering the world, and has been a powerful presence in the New World for six decades, ruling over countless territories in the New World, namely her kingdom of Totto Land, and being a powerful and influential figure in the Underworld . Linlin's crew believed she would be the one to claim the One Piece and become the new Pirate King, and she had the ambition to do so. Charlotte Katakuri is the son of Big Mom, a member of the Charlotte Family , and a high ranking member of the Big Mom Pirates ; being one of her Three Sweet Commanders. Widely considered to be the strongest member of the crew save for her, Katakuri has frequently taken charge over his crewmates and siblings. He governs over Komugi Island as its minister. He also built up a reputation that led to family members idolizing him to a great degree. Boa Hancock is the captain of the Kuja Pirates and the empress of Amazon Lily , being beloved by men and women alike for her unparalleled beauty. Her first voyage as a pirate was so impactful that she received a high bounty and an invitation to join the Seven Warlords immediately afterwards. Donquixote Doflamingo is the captain of the Donquixote Pirates , whose current executives notably started following him at a young age specifically because he possessed Supreme King Haki. He also formerly ruled Dressrosa as its king for the past decade and accumulated great power as a broker in the Underworld under the codename "Joker". In addition, he was born a World Noble , and is the only of his direct biological family to remain attached to the title after his parents renounced the Nobles. Kaidou is the governor-general of the Beasts Pirates , and was formerly one of the Four Emperors who ruled over territory in the New World. He is regarded as the "Strongest Creature" in the world and previously ruled over Wano for 25 years, with the island of Onigashima as his base of operations. He allied with Charlotte Linlin with the goal of conquering the world. His crew believed he would be the one to claim the One Piece and become the new Pirate King, and his second in command at one point believed him to be Joy Boy . Yamato is the son of Kaidou and used to be known by the epithet "Oni Princess". In addition, he had made the decision early in life to become Kouzuki Oden, who also possessed Supreme King Haki and kingly qualities of his own. Eustass Kid is the captain of the Kid Pirates . Having possessed the highest bounty among Worst Generation members for a large portion of the group's existence, he has constantly demonstrated an unbreakable drive to reach the top. Kouzuki Oden was the heir to the shogunate of Wano Country , an independent nation renowned for its military strength. He conquered the country's lawless region of Kuri , leading to his appointment to the position of its daimyo. Oden was also the leader of the notoriously strong group of samurai known as the Nine Red Scabbards and inspired their future rebellion to overthrow the tyrannical leader of Wano. In addition to this, he was the second division commander of the Whitebeard Pirates and sailed alongside Gol D. Roger at the end of his journey, meaning he visited Laugh Tale . Roronoa Zoro is one of the most renowned members of the Straw Hat Pirates having the goal of becoming the Strongest Swordsman in the World. Onlookers who have witnessed his strength and willpower have expressed surprise at Zoro not being the Straw Hats' captain, leading them to wonder how powerful Luffy is. With his captain now currently one of the Four Emperors, the world currently recognizes Zoro as one of the senior officers of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet . Chinjao was the leader of the powerful and infamous Happo Navy , being considered to be a rival to the Marine Monkey D. Garp and Gol D. Roger while in their prime. Saints Topman Warcury , Marcus Mars , Jaygarcia Saturn , and Ethanbaron V. Nusjuro are World Nobles and members of the Five Elders , the highest public authority in the World Government . Joy Boy was a legendary figure from the Void Century and believed to be the world's first pirate . Scopper Gaban is a former member of the Roger Pirates who was the "Left Hand" of the Pirate King and is known by his epithet "Mountain Eater". Nerona Imu is the King of the World ( 世界の王 , Sekai no Ō ) , one of the First Twenty , and the secret supreme ruler of the World Government. He is also the occupant of the Empty Throne . Rocks D. Xebec was the captain of the Rocks Pirates , and had ambitions to become King of the World ( 世界の王 , Sekai no Ō ) . Harald was the king of Elbaph and a descendant of the Ancient Giants . Loki is the prince of Elbaph and a son of Harald, and like his father, a descendant of the Ancient Giants. He also expressed himself to be the sun god Nika and expressed a desire to destroy the world. Among non-canon Supreme King Haki users, Naguri was a former pirate captain, while Douglas Bullet , though never a conventional leader, constantly worked to get stronger for the purpose of taking over the world and defeating every global power to achieve the title of Pirate King. While this is shown to be a very rare gift among the world as a whole, it is noted by Don Chinjao that the New World is filled with numerous others who possess the ability to use this Haki. He explains that Luffy will most certainly encounter them and fight them for the title of Pirate King. His words prove true, as Luffy would face more Supreme King Haki users in the New World, each proving to be an extremely powerful opponent he needed to defeat in order to accomplish his goal at the time. Training Awakening Methods It is currently unknown if there is an actual way to awaken this Haki, since it cannot be learned, only controlled after one discovers that they are one in several million people to be born with it. So far, all seen cases of people awakening their Supreme King Haki involved them using the ability subconsciously while in a state of distress, but also with an unwavering will. Training Methods Silvers Rayleigh has stated that although this Haki can be controlled, it cannot be trained to become stronger directly. Since it is, in essence, the embodiment of the user's spirit, it can only become stronger if the user themselves becomes stronger. Anime and Manga Differences In the manga, those hit with Supreme King Haki foam at the mouth after or while losing consciousness, whereas, in the anime, they simply faint. Later on in the anime, Supreme King Haki has varying depictions between episodes of the series and films: In later episodes, the use of Supreme King Haki causes the affected area, excluding the user, to be highlighted in a dark blue tint, presumably for dramatic effect. In Episode 723 , the user is depicted with a dark purple aura surrounding them. In One Piece Film: Red , the affected area, including the user, turns monochrome and flashes red, with the effect slowly dissolving as the user stops their emission. In One Piece Film: Z and One Piece: Stampede , the Supreme King Haki's user creates a circular wave that expands and engulfs their surroundings. The former being purple, and in the latter's case a color unique to each user: Luffy's being red, and Bullet's being dark blue. Throughout the Wano Country Saga , Supreme King Haki has a gold sparkling effect visually similar to the Pika Pika no Mi . In the case of Roger and Whitebeard, their pupils also shine with a red glow. Translation and Dub Issues The term Haō ( 覇王 ) in Haōshoku is commonly translated as either "Supreme King/Ruler" or "Conqueror" and refers to a person of high/ruler status who subjugates lesser lords and governs with military might and strategy (hegemony) rather than virtue. The term can be distinguished from the more generic hasha ( 覇者 ) . Funimation refers to it as "Conqueror's Spirit" throughout the Summit War Saga , the term stemming from it being the only type of Haki that cannot be awakened or strengthened through training.

For the chapter of the same name, see Chapter 369 . Ramen Kenpo is a martial arts style used by Wanze . Overview Ramen Kenpo consists of the user using self-made ramen noodles to fight. To do this, Wanze swallows large amounts of flour, mixes it with his saliva to make it into dough and extrudes it from of his nose, using his nose hairs to cut the dough into noodles for either battle or cooking. Because of this, Sanji, Usopp, and Franky find Wanze's dishes disgusting and refuse to eat them. He uses a special flour called Alloy Flour ( 合金小麦粉 , Gōkin Komugiko ) to make the dough which causes the noodles to be has hard as metal. The style also makes use of other tools and weapons, such as Wanze's roller skates and kitchen knives, showing that he does not follow the same "kitchen tools are not for killing" policy as Sanji . Sanji finds that Wanze's style of cooking and fighting to be a waste of food and a disrespect to cooking utensils, and thus makes him unqualified of being a cook. Techniques Ramen Beam ( 拉麺 ビーム , Rāmen Bīmu , literally meaning "Japanese Noodle Beam") : Wanze's signature technique. He first swallows a large amount of flour, then covers one of his nostrils and fires it out of the other in the form of hard, sharpened noodles that pierce his target. When using his "Men's Formal Suit", this attack serves as a counter against any attacks directed against his unarmored upper body. Men-Kiri ( 麺切り , Men-Kiri , literally meaning "Noodle Cutting") : Wanze strikes his roller skates against a surface repeatedly until the friction sets them on fire. In the VIZ Manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Noodle Slicer . Fire Skate ( 炎包丁 , Faia Sukēto , literally meaning "Flaming Cleaver") : After using "Men-Kiri", Wanze jumps and performs a flaming, sweeping kick with his ignited roller skates. This is called Fire Skate Blades in the Viz Manga and Funimation dub but keeps the original name in the subs. Ramen Kenpo Ogi: Men's Formal Suit ( ラーメン拳法「奥義」 麺 ズ 正裝 スーツ , Rāmen Kenpō Ōgi: Menzu Fōmaru Sūtsu , literally meaning "Ramen Kenpo Ultimate Technique: Japanese Noodle Formal Battle-Suit") : Wanze swallows an entire sack of flour and uses it to produce a large amount of noodles from his nose, which he shapes into a giant noodle armor for him to wear. Besides serving as form of defensive protection and increasing the force of his blows, if any opponents try to strike him, they will be caught between the noodles, rendering them vulnerable to other counteroffensive attacks. Menn in this case means "noodle", but is written so that it is a pun with the English word "men". In the Viz Manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Ra-Men's Formal Suit . Tonkotsu Spank ( 豚骨スパンク , Tonkotsu Supanku , literally meaning "Pork Bone Spank") : A straight punch using the noodle armor's arm. If the opponent tries to deflect the punch, they will be stuck in the noodles, with Wanze then smashing them around his surroundings. Tonkotsu is a ramen broth made from pork bones. In the Viz Manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Pork Bone Spank . Men Soul Press ( 麺魂 プレス , Men Sōru Puresu , literally meaning "Noodle Soul Press") : Once he has an opponent stuck in his noodles, Wanze jumps and crashes the entire noodle armor down on them with a massive body slam. This is called Soul Man Pasta Press in the Viz Manga and Noodle Soul Press in the Funimation adaptations. Ramen Gyoza Set: Clapping ( ラーメンギョーザセット クラッピング , Rāmen Gyōza Setto: Kurappingu , literally meaning "Japanese Noodle Potsticker Set - Clapping") : Wanze claps the arms of his noodle armor one against the other, attempting to catch his opponent between them. This is called Ramen and Potsticker Combo: Super Slap in the Viz Manga, Ramen and Pot Sticker Combo: Clapping in the Funimation subs, and Ramen and Gyoza Combo, Clapping in the dub. Fukumen Spank ( 複麺スパンク , Fukumen Supanku , literally meaning "Double-Noodle Spank") : Wanze splits the arms of his noodle armor into several smaller ones and launches a barrage of punches towards his opponent. In the Viz Manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Multi-Noodle Spank . Senmen Muchi ( 千麺ムチ , Senmen Muchi , literally meaning "Thousand-Noodle Whip") : Wanze swings the noodle armor's arms like whips, the arms now merely being long strings of noodle without "fists". In the Viz Manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Thousand Noodle Whip . Menkui Knocker ( 麺杭ノッカー , Menkui Nokkā , literally meaning "Noodle-Stake Knocker") : Wanze jumps in the air and sends both of his noodle armor's massive legs flying towards the opponent, with the ramen forming a sharp point at the end. This is called Noodle Nail Knockout in the Viz Manga and Funimation dub and Noodle Skate Knocker in the subs. Hocho Nage ( 包丁投げ , Hōchō Nage , literally meaning "Kitchen-Knife Throw") : Wanze throws several kitchen knives at his opponent. This is called Knife Throw in the Viz Manga and Kitchen Knife Throw in the Funimation adaptations. Ramen Kenpo Ura Hiden: Men-Kiri Doku Bocho ( ラーメン拳法「裏秘伝」麺切り毒包丁 , Rāmen Kenpō Ura Hiden: Men-Kiri Doku Bōchō , literally meaning "Japanese Noodle Most Secret Technique: Noodle-Cutting Poisonous Kitchen-Knife") : Wanze attacks his opponent with a gigantic kitchen knife coated in deadly poison , said to cause certain death with a single scratch. This is called Ramen Kung Fu — Secret Attack!!! Poison Pasta Cutter!!! in the Viz Manga, Ramen Kenpo Secret Technique Noodle Cutter Poisonous Kitchen Knife in the Funimation subs, and Ramen Kenpo Secret Move: Poison Pasta Cutter in the dub.

as its king for the past decade and accumulated great power as a under the codename "Joker". In addition, he was born a , and is the only of his direct to remain attached to the title after his parents renounced the Nobles. , and was formerly one of the Four Emperors who ruled over territory in the New World. He is regarded as the "Strongest Creature" in the world and previously ruled over Wano for 25 years, with the island of as his base of operations. He allied with Charlotte Linlin with the goal of conquering the world. His crew believed he would be the one to claim the One Piece and become the new Pirate King, and his

The Deri Deri no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to deliver an object to any target within view, making the user a Delivery Human ( デリバリー人間 , Deribarī ningen ) . It was eaten by Prize . Etymology "Deri" comes from Deribarī , the Japanese pronunciation of "Delivery". Strengths and Weaknesses This fruit's abilities are rather strong, as it gives the user the ability to target any object it can view and deliver anything right to it. This includes delivering things that are too small or invisible for the user to see, like a virus. The fruit also allows the user to deliver others' hearts to themselves, similar to Law 's ability . The things being delivered will stop if the user is knocked unconscious, and they will lose momentum and fall from the air. The fruit's user is also susceptible to standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Prize used his abilities to throw various items, including spears, a virus, and the poison gas bomb created by Caesar Clown . He also used it to remove Caesar and, later, Igaram Jr. 's hearts to manipulate for his own advantage. Techniques Spear Delivery ( 槍デリバリー , Yari deribarī ) : Prize throws a spear at his opponents. It was first used against his subordinates after they failed to retrieve Caesar. With this technique, Prize is also able to remove the hearts of his opponents. Virus Delivery ( ウィルス・デリバリー , U~irusu deribarī ) : Prize throws a virus at his opponents, causing them to cough uncontrollably. It was first used against the Straw Hat Pirates during his first confrontation with them. Delivery!! Max! ( デリバリー!!マックス! , Deribarī! ! Makkusu! ) : Prize uses his most concentrated delivery throw to launch one of Caesar's poison gas bombs. It was first used against an evacuation ship of Kings from the Levely .

The Zushi Zushi no Mi is a Paramecia -type Devil Fruit which allows the user to create and manipulate gravitational force. It was eaten by Issho , better known as Admiral Fujitora. Etymology Zushizushi ( ずしずし ) is a Japanese onomatopoeia that describes a "thump" or "thud" sound, representing the footfall of something heavy (e.g. stomps of sumo wrestlers). Strengths and Weaknesses Whoever eats from this fruit is granted the power to create gravitational forces of extreme potency. The ability user is able to move about living things and objects with little effort, ranging from debris to send flying against an enemy in smaller or massive numbers, to a massive battleship . The range of the generable gravity field appears to be massive, as it can pull down meteors from the highest of skies (possibly the outer atmosphere, or even space itself). Able to have their gravity go in any direction, even horizontal, as demonstrated by Fujitora, the user can move about matter with precision, effectively granting a form of telekinesis. By negating natural gravity and forcing things upward to navigate in midair, flight can be simulated, as shown by Fujitora making either himself fly on a small rock platform or levitate himself alongside his men on a ship. He is also able to manipulate multiple different gravity fields simultaneously, as seen when fighting Luffy at the end of Dressrosa Arc , where he kept vast amounts of rubble floating across an island-wide scale while attacking him with offensive gravity wells. However, it seems that his gravity powers can be disrupted by attacking Fujitora himself. This was shown when Roronoa Zoro used a projectile slash to interrupt the admiral from keeping him held in a gravitational pin, enabling Zoro to escape from it. Also, people and objects are capable of resisting the gravity if they are physically strong enough to maintain a hold on their current location. In addition, it seems that the user is incapable of affecting themselves with the fruit; thus, they must create a platform to stand on in order to achieve flight. Other than all that, the user is also affected by the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Fujitora can create gravitational forces (portrayed as circles of transparent energy, purple-colored in the anime, connecting to his epithet) of various intensity, direction, and purpose, especially combat-oriented, and usually unsheathes his sword to do so (suggesting a possible, so-far unspecified connection between the weapon and his powers). Prominently in battle, Fujitora employs downforces of gravity to press opposition against the ground with varying degrees of power, from using such heavy force as to crack, cave in, and deeply crater the earth as well as keep physical powerhouses fully restrained (or just barely able to move, let alone stand) to using just enough gravity to keep a physically weakened person pinned down and immobilized. Another feat that is seen done repeatedly by the admiral is the summoning of meteorites from high up in the sky (though unknown from how far), even if just as a "test" of his abilities. Apart from that, Fujitora can reverse the flow of gravity to create an attack-repelling field around him, akin to a circular barrier. He can also, by negating the normal downforce of gravity, levitate free-lying objects around him and control them, even incredibly huge amounts. Although this power of levitation resembles that of the Fuwa Fuwa no Mi used by Shiki , Fujitora is not hindered by the necessity of having to touch a given target beforehand, and he can target both the living and inanimate. Techniques Gravito ( 重力刀 , Gurabitō , literally meaning "Gravity Blade") : Fujitora's signature technique. Fujitora can manipulate gravitational forces by channeling his Devil Fruit's ability through his sword, sending gravitational forces by swinging or just simply moving his weapon. The direction of the gravitational forces can be upwards, downwards, or even sidewards, usually just one at a time. The technique was first used against thugs of the Donquixote Pirates by generating a deeply ground-cratering downforce of gravity. However, this technique was not named until his fight against Sabo . This is called Gravity Blade in the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations. When paired with the subsequent attack Moko , it is spelled with hiragana instead of katakana, in the form " 重力刀 ". Moko ( 猛虎 , Mōko , literally meaning "Fierce Tiger") : Fujitora generates immense levels of gravitational forces around him that push out horizontally, which knock away anything in its path, including his enemies, buildings, trees, and whatever else that is unfortunate enough to be in the attack radius. The force is so strong that it easily destroys the ground and buildings, reducing them to rubble. While using this technique, the gravitational force generates black lightning as a visual effect around Fujitora. It was first used against Sabo. This is called Raging Tiger in the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations. The name of this technique is a reference to Issho's epithet, Fujitora, which means "wisteria tiger"; and likely to shogi as well, as "Mōko" refers to two different pieces pronounced the same way, being "the blind tiger" (盲虎) and "the fierce tiger" (猛虎), the latter allowing one to move two squares forward in some variants . Non-Canon Techniques Jigoku Tabi ( 地獄旅 , Jigoku Tabi , literally meaning "Hell Journey") : Fujitora slices through a large group of enemies over a wide area, then as he sheathes his sword it causes a powerful downward force of gravity sending them down into a massive crater. Its name comes from Zatoichi and the Chess Expert ( 座頭市地獄旅 , Zatōichi Jigoku-tabi ) . It is dubbed Trip to Hell in Pirate Warriors 3 . Fujitora has used a form of this attack in the manga to force opponents into a crater, but he does not slice through them to do so unlike in the anime and Pirate Warriors 3 . Abare Himatsuri ( あばれ火祭り , Abare Himatsuri , literally meaning "Raving Fire Festival") : Fujitora stands on a piece of rock and levitates it into the air and flies across the battlefield, raining a storm of meteorites in his wake. Its name comes from Zatoichi Goes to the Fire Festival ( 座頭市あばれ火祭り , Zatoichi Abare Himatsuri ) It is used and dubbed as Festival of Furious Flames in Pirate Warriors 3 .

Kanpo Kenpo is a form of martial art used by Nightin . Overview The fighting revolves around using powdered herbal medicine and poison to boost one's strength or to create painful inflictions on the enemies, thrown and applied in various manners. Various types of medicine also be used for various auxiliary effects, which can even rejuvenate one's youth or serve as a physical barrier and weapon. Techniques Kanpo Kenpo: Gedokusan ( 解毒剤 , Kanpō Kenpō: Gedokusan , literally meaning "Chinese Medicine Martial Art: Antidote Granule") : Nightin spins at great speed while releasing one of her many medicinal mixes creating a tornado that sends flying everything around her. This is called Kampo Kenpo: Antidote Granule in the Funimation adaptations. Kanpo Kenpo Hiyaku: Dokudami Jigoku ( 漢方拳法秘薬毒痛み地獄 , Kanpō Kenpō Hiyaku: Dokudami Jigoku , literally meaning "Chinese Medicine Martial Art Nostrum: Fishwort Hell") : Nightin mixes up and consumes an extremely bitter powder made of hundreds of bitter-tasting medicinal herbs. This technique was used to shock her into not being affected by Boa Hancock 's Mero Mero Mellow . This is called Kampo Kenpo Secret Medicine: Fishwort Hell in the Funimation adaptations. Kanpo Doku Kiri ( 漢方毒霧 , Kanpō Doku Kiri , literally meaning "Chinese Medicine Poison Mist") : Nightin drinks a bottle of poison before spitting it at her opponent as a mist. This mist is acidic and dissolves clothing, creating a distraction for her opponent to keep themselves covered up. This is called Kampo Poison Mist in the Funimation adaptations. Kanpo Kenpo Hiyaku: Korai Shinjin ( 漢方拳法高麗新人 , Kanpō Kenpō Hiyaki: Kōrai Shinjin , literally meaning "Chinese Medicine Martial Art Nostrum: Ginseng Newcomer") : Nightin swallows a powdered mixture which has the effect of transforming her, making her body taller, younger, and stronger. This is called Kampo Kempo Secret Medicine: Ginseng Newcomer in the Funimation subs. In this form, Nightin can use the real fighting skills of the Kanpo Kenpo: Kanpo Kenpo Ogi: Kakkon Toe Kick ( 漢方拳法奥義葛根トウキック , Kanpō Kenpō Ōgi: Kakkon Tou Kikku , literally meaning "Chinese Medicine Martial Art Secret Technique: Kudzu Root Toe Kick") : Nightin runs at her opponent and delivers several kicks at close range. When Nightin names this attack she usually repeats "Toe" every time she kicks. The name of this attack is a pun as "Kakkon Toe" sounds like "kakkontou" a Chinese medicine term for the infusion of kudzu, cinnamon and other components used as an anti-febrile. This is called Kampo Kenpo Technique: Kakkon Toe-Kick in the Funimation adaptations. Tochuka Soul Shot ( 杜仲科ソウルショット , Tōchūka Souru Shotto , literally meaning "Eucommia Soul Shot") : Nightin throws an extremely strong strike with the end of the fingers of her hand in her rejuvenated form. This attack is powerful enough to destroy a wood stairs with ease. This is called Cordyceps Soul Shot in the Funimation adaptations. Tochuka Soul Shot Burst ( 杜仲科ソウルショットバースト , Tōchūka Souru Shotto Bāsuto , literally meaning Eucommia Soul Shot Burst) : Nightin throws many punches and palm strikes in rapid succession, creating the illusion that she has many arms. This is called Cordyceps Soul Shot Burst in the Funimation adaptations. Kanpo Dome ( 漢方ドーム , Kanpō Dōmu , literally meaning "Chinese Medicine Dome") : Nightin throws powdered medicine into the air in front of her as a shield. It was effective to counter Hancock's Slave Arrow and Pistol Kiss . This is called Kampo Dome in the Funimation adaptations.

The Shuku Shuku no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to compress things, making the user a Compression Human ( 圧縮人間 , Asshuku Ningen ) . It was eaten by Bolster . Etymology "Shuku" is derived from asshuku ( 圧縮 ) meaning "compression". Strengths and Weaknesses The fruit's main strength that it allows the user to compress large objects into a more convenient size. This can be weaponized, for example, by compressing large bombs into a small size. The user can also compress intangible force or energy and then release it as a shock wave. If the user receives damage, the compressed objects will return normal. Otherwise, the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses apply. Usage Bolster mainly uses this ability for combat.

ability_name: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Phoenix
ability_description: For other models of this fruit, see Tori Tori no Mi (Disambiguation) . The Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Phoenix is a Mythical Zoan -type Devil Fruit that grants the power to transform into a phoenix and half-phoenix hybrid at will, as well as self-regenerate (and heal others) using blue phoenix flames. The fruit was eaten by Marco . Etymology Tori ( 鳥 ) is Japanese for "bird". The kanji of the "Model" designation read fushichō ( 不死鳥 , lit. "immortal bird") , a Japanese title for the Western phoenix, and are glossed with the katakana for fenikkusu ( フェニックス ) , the common Japanese pronunciation of "phoenix" (borrowed from English, but based on the Latin pronunciation of the word). Appearance The Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Phoenix resembles a pineapple : an oval fruit with green skin segmented into round fruitlets, which each feature a signature Devil Fruit swirl in the middle and come to a prickly point on top. Instead of the T-shaped stem typically seen with Devil Fruits, this fruit possesses a crown of long, blade-shaped leaves that stiffly point up, form the occasional Devil Fruit swirl, and overall resemble the blue-yellow phoenix flames that this fruit grants access to. Transformations The ability user can transform into a mythical phoenix: a large, vaguely peafowl -shaped bird that is completely shrouded in blue (partially yellow) flames. This firebird form comes with two sizeable wings featuring eyespot feathers, a long and slender neck, a yellow-flamed head crest, legs with sharp talons, and a trio of long, yellow tail feathers made of closed, bound-together circles. Notably through Marco's use, no full-bodied Human-Beast Form has been seen (that evenly spreads over his entire body), only partial morphing of his arms and legs into wings and bird legs respectively; whether or not this partial transformation is in fact this Zoan's default Human-Beast Form remains unconfirmed. Beast Form. Human-Beast Form. Strengths As with all Zoans, the ability user's transformations greatly bolster their physical abilities, granting (in Marco's case) strength high enough to hold back two powerful Ancient Zoan users simultaneously. As a bird-type Zoan , it bestows the power of flight in both the Human-Beast and Beast Form, allowing the user to excel in matters of combat mobility and travel. Either one of the user's phoenix forms also comes with sharp sets of talons on the feet that can deliver slashing damage via kicks. The special "mythical" trait this Zoan confers upon the user is the ability to summon the blue flames of the phoenix—dubbed the "Blue Flames of Resurrection" ( 復活の青い炎 , Fukkatsu no Aoi Honō , VIZ: Blue Flame of Revival) and "Flames of Regeneration" ( 再生の炎 , Saisei no Honō , Funimation: Flames of Restoration) . The user is automatically clad in these flames upon transforming, but doing so is not strictly necessary for the flames to be conjured, at least in smaller amounts. Notably, while the phoenix flames do not burn, spread, or exude heat the way mundane fire does, their "special" nature makes them comparatively stronger; during a clash between Marco and Big Mom , Marco was able to blow away and cause pain to Prometheus , a living fire homie born from Big Mom's super-strong soul . Rather than burn, the phoenix flames have extraordinary healing power. When cloaked in them, the user can rapidly regenerate all injuries inflicted onto them—even mortal ones—much like a phoenix rising anew from its ashes. This makes the user enormously resilient and able to undergo extreme punishment without feeling pain or being slowed down. In Marco's case, devastating injuries like being riddled with bullets, having his head and torso run through with blades, chunks of his head and neck being blown off, or one of his wings being severed all have elicited little reaction from him, the flames erasing the damage about as quickly as it occurs. The ability user can enter their phoenix form to automatically heal all prior-accumulated damage in one go, or else heal by summoning the flames in isolation, without having to transform. In full phoenix mode, non- Haki attacks may simply pass through the user's body as though it were pure flame, in a way superficially resembling a Logia 's invulnerability; the defining difference is that the user merely regenerates and retains a tangible, solid body beneath the fire. Thus, even injuries sustained under Seastone contact can be healed by the flames once contact with said material stops. In battle, the phoenix flames provide exceptional defense, their constant healing effect allowing the user to ward off extremely powerful attacks by using their own body—notably their large wings—as a shield. In doing so, Marco has successfully blocked and stood firm against overwhelming assaults like Marine Admiral Kizaru 's volley of light bullets , Admiral Akainu 's scorching magma fist , or the Emperor Kaidou 's castle-leveling breath of dragon fire . Moreover, while the blue flames lack the raw destructiveness of natural fire, they can still be wielded offensively, as Marco demonstrated by using fire-based attacks that, instead of burning, create powerful shockwaves and/or deliver blunt-force strikes that send opponents flying. Furthermore, any part of the phoenix body that is separated from the main body, like a wing, remains under the user's control, allowing it to be reshaped and used in a counterattack. Beyond self-recovery, the phoenix flames can be applied to treat others, even large groups of people if the user manually spreads out their fire in a large wave. When cast over someone else's injuries, the flames speed up the natural healing process to promote faster recovery, e.g. fixing a broken limb so that it is fully functional the next day. Once the flames have lit other people, they stay with and continuously heal them, the recipients' stamina permitting. This may even extend to diseases, as shown when Marco employed his fire as a temporary countermeasure against Queen the Plague 's cold-inducing virus , raising the body heat of all infected parties caught by his flames to halt the virus' spread and progression. That said, the ability user cannot mend others as well as they can their own body, the healing having a much smaller and slower effect on others. Weaknesses A downside of the phoenix flames is that, being purely for healing, they have none of the inherent attack potency (heat and ability to burn) of regular fire. However, they can still be used offensively to some degree and, owing to their "special" nature, can be used neutralize normal fire. While the user's regeneration can nullify most attacks, it has a limit. That is, the user is not truly immortal, suggesting that damage beyond a certain threshold (sustained all at once, over time, or both) might overwhelm the healing factor. Regardless, Haki is established as a reliable counter to the regeneration: while attempts at healing or defense are still possible, the damage from Haki-infused attacks persists. This was first demonstrated at Marineford where, despite ignoring hits from gunfire, a flying Marco was easily punched out of the air by Vice Admiral Garp , presumably using Haki. During the Summit War and later the Raid on Onigashima , despite Marco instantly healing fatal wounds upon receiving them, he still accrued some minor damage over time, like scrapes and bruises, especially after facing more powerful foes; in the aftermath of either conflict, Marco was seen in bandages, hinting at injuries that he could or chose not to heal. Related to this, the regeneration may be slower if the user is caught off-guard or not transformed, as seen when Marco was pierced by Kizaru's lasers in his Human Form but did not instantly heal, despite the wounds' severity. Lastly, while the user can shrug off damage from attacks, they remain susceptible to restraint, e.g. being grabbed and held in place. The phoenix flames are known to draw upon the user's stamina, making prolonged use of them physically taxing until the flames can no longer be effectively created. On Onigashima , after fighting two powerful opponents for an extended period and having previously used his flames on dozens of other people, Marco was temporarily put out of commission, which onlookers attributed to his stamina being depleted. This also holds true for when the phoenix flames are used on others, being not as strong in their healing as when applied to the user's own body and fading once the recipient's stamina has run out. The flames' limited effect on others is also evident from how Marco was unable to help his elderly captain Whitebeard , who normally had to be hooked up to medical machinery as a result of his declining health. Otherwise, the user suffers from the standard weaknesses of any Devil Fruit user, like Seastone fully sealing their powers upon contact. Usage Marco's abilities are well-known and have garnered him the epithet Marco the Phoenix . He extensively relies on his transformation for aerial combat, and thanks to its physical and "mythical" qualities, his powers are highly versatile, allowing him to excel in offense, defense, and support roles during battle. Marco's bird physiology allow him to fly at high speeds and utilize his human legs to deliver strong Haki-imbued kicks or knee strikes, and his phoenix legs to land slashing talon strikes. Marco can further employ his wings as a means of restraining foes, even extremely strong and fully transformed Ancient Zoan users, as seen when Marco easily subdued King and Queen —two of the strongest members of the Beasts Pirates with one billion-plus bounties —at once. For direct offense, Marco can shape his flames into shockwave-like blasts that, rather than burn opponents, deal concussive damage to or send them flying. In combat, Marco's regeneration serves a passive, defensive purpose, enabling him to outright ignore most physical attacks he is faced with. Ranged and melee blows, even weapons, pass right through him while any wounds received heal near-instantly, closing up without leaving a scar; this may make it seem even to formidable adversaries that Marco is invincible. Among other things, he has been observed shielding his allies using his own body and flames before simply healing himself up afterwards. In terms of healing others, Marco's flames have shown immense usefulness on battlefields with a lot of potential allies to be mended, whether from injury or infection. For years, Marco served as an efficient ship doctor for the Whitebeard Pirates , and following the crew's disbandment, he went on to do doctoring on Sphinx with his powers. Marco has made practical use of flying to traverse battlefields quickly and bypass enemies on the ground, in order to supervise whatever fighting is going on (as he did during the Summit War of Marineford in Whitebeard 's stead), to relay important intel, and to carry others with him. In his apprentice days, Marco acted as a scout for the Whitebeard Pirates, investigating unknown islands. Marco can seamlessly switch between his Beast or Human-Beast Forms. Unlike most Zoan users, in his half-phoenix form, he undergoes a more selective transformation, usually shifting just his arms into phoenix wings and his legs into phoenix legs, while his head and torso remain unchanged. If this is a mark of Marco's superior control over his powers or the Human-Beast Form's default look is unclear. Techniques Fujiazami ( 不 死 薊 , Fujiazami , literally meaning "Immortal Thistle") : A defensive move. Marco in phoenix mode jumps in front of someone he wishes to protect and, while midair, forms his wings into a shield-like vortex of flames to block an incoming attack. Being Marco's first-seen technique, Fujiazami was used by him (but not named) when protecting Whitebeard from Kizaru during the Summit War , warding off the admiral 's Yasakani no Magatama . It was named during the battle at Onigashima when Marco used the technique to intercept King 's Andon attack, shielding a bandaged-up Roronoa Zoro . Later on, the technique proved strong enough to block Kaidou 's Bolo Breath . The move's name is a pun on fujiazami ( 富士薊 ) , the name of the Japanese Fuji thistle . In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is named Undying Thistle . Hoo-in ( 鳳 凰 印 , Hōō-in , literally meaning "Fènghuáng Seal") : An aerial attack where Marco readies his phoenix talons in front of him in a seal-like pose, then delivers a devastating two-legged kick that causes a fiery shockwave. When first demonstrated, the move was strong enough to knock back the Queen Mama Chanter , a colossal ship (which in the anime involved Marco using a single leg and leaving a talon-like imprint on the ship). Later when used against Queen , Hoo-in was sent out from a distance as a fireball, striking the All-Star 's brachiosaurus neck with enough force to making him vomit blood. This attack's name contains the Japanese name for the fènghuáng ( hōō ), an immortal bird originating in Chinese mythology that, being often compared to the Western phoenix, is sometimes called "Chinese phoenix" (despite mythological similarities between the two being only superficial). In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this technique is called Phoenix Brand . Nashi no Tsubute ( 鳳梨 礫 , Nashi no Tsubute , literally meaning "Pineapple Stone") : A support technique where Marco whirls his phoenix wings around to disperse his flames over a wide area, allowing him to set alight and heal multiple people. He used this to counteract Queen 's Ice Oni virus in those infected on the Skull Dome's Live Floor , keeping the flames at just the right temperature to slow the virus' effects. However, the flames draw on the stamina of recipients, ceasing to work once it is depleted. The technique's name is a twofold pun: the kanji include hōri ( 鳳梨 ) , an antiquated Japanese word for pineapple, which plays on Marco's supposed likeness to said fruit and itself contains the kanji hō ( 鳳 ) for "fèng" (a fènghuáng's male aspect); however, the furigana indicate the reading Nashi no Tsubute ( 梨の礫 ) , which is an idiom that literally means "tossing the pear" and refers to a lack of reply after a question (the "nashi" for "pear" sounds the same as "nashi" for "nothing"). In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Phoenix Pyreapple . Bluebird ( 青炎雁 , Burūbādo , literally meaning "Blue-Flamed Goose") : An attack where Marco, using an outstretched index and middle finger, remotely shapes his phoenix flames into a dense, swirling mass in front of him, then launches it straight towards his enemy in a thrust-like, concussive strike. When performing Bluebird against King, Marco used one of his cut-off wings as a medium for the fireball, producing it from the wing's feathers and slamming it into King with immense force, sending the All-Star flying. In a later (anime-only) use of the attack against Queen, the flames morphed into a firebird, which flapped its wings as it flew toward the All-Star but was easily sliced apart by Queen with his sword. The bluebird is a passerine bird named for its blue plumage. The attack's name may also reference (given the kanji for "goose") how in Chinese myth, especially per ancient texts, the fènghuáng is said to bear various animal resemblances, e.g. having a goose's breast. Ongle ( 鶴爪 , Onguru , literally meaning "Crane Claw") : A melee attack similar to Hoo-in . Marco performs a flying, single-legged kick at an opponent while his talons do slashing damage. This kick proved strong enough to blow the All-Star Queen away with great force, while gashing his face. Ongle is French for "nail". The attack's name (the kanji) may play on how the fènghuáng is said, per modern descriptions, to be the composite of various birds, including having a crane's legs. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Crane Talons . Non-Canon Techniques Power Leg Claw ( パワーレッグクロー , Pawā Reggu Kurō ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco kicks his opponent with his talons while in his Human-Beast Form. Power Leg Spin ( パワーレッグスピン , Pawā Reggu Supin ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco performs a roundhouse kick with his talons while in his Human-Beast Form. Power Leg Shoot ( パワーレッグシュート , Pawā Reggu Shūto ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco delivers a sweeping kick with his talons while in his Human-Beast Form. Phoenix Spear ( フェニックススピア , Fenikkusu Supia ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco flies forward in his Human-Beast Form to deliver a flying kick to his opponent. Dive Attack ( ダイブアタック , Daibu Atakku ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , Marco while in his Human-Beast Form divebombs into his opponent from above, then shifts into Human Form to repeatedly strike them with his bare hands. Air Raid Smash ( エアーレイドスマッシュ , Eā Reido Sumasshu ) : An attack exclusive to Unlimited Cruise: SP , where Marco turns into his Human-Beast Form, spins around to draw his opponent in, kicks them into the air, flies up to them, and then kicks them back down to the ground. Saisei no Hono ( 再生の炎 , Saisei no Honō , literally meaning "Flames of Regeneration") : Used as his Level 1 Special Attack in Pirate Warriors 2 and 3 , where Marco changes into a phoenix and flaps his wings, restoring some of his health while sending out a shockwave. The technique reappears in Pirate Warriors 4 under the same name. In the games' English releases, the technique is called Rebirth Flame (in 2 and 3 ) and Flame of Restoration (in 4 ). Tensei no Soen ( 転生の蒼炎 , Tensei no Sōen , literally meaning "Blue Flame of Transmigration") : Marco changes into a phoenix, spirals around enemies to gather them into the air, then charges into the ground from a very high altitude, unleashing a shockwave. It is used in Pirate Warriors 2 and 3 as his Level 2 Special Attack, it reappears as a Special Attack for Marco in Pirate Warriors 4 . In the games' English releases, the technique is called Blue Transmigration Flame (in 2 and 3 ) and Transmigration Flame (in 4 ). Fushicho no Goen ( 不死鳥の豪炎 , Fushichō no Gōen , literally meaning "Phoenix's Great Flame") : While airborne in his Human-Beast Form, Marco shoots a fiery shockwave forward from his wings, suffering some slight recoil in the process. The move is seen in Pirate Warriors 3 as Marco's Kizuna Rush Special Attack and likewise reappears in Pirate Warriors 4 , as part of Marco's Special Attack set. In the game's English releases, the move is called Phoenix Inferno ( 3 ) and Phoenix Ignition ( 4 ). Fushicho no Mezame ( 不死鳥の目覚め , Fushichō no Mezame , literally meaning "Phoenix's Awakening") : Featured in Pirate Warriors 4 , this is Marco's Serious Burst mode, which causes him to remain floating in the air in his Human-Beast Form (and is activated while midair). In the game's English release, the technique is called Phoenix Awakening . Fushicho no Yokugeki ( 不死鳥の翼撃 , Fushichō no Mezame , literally meaning "Phoenix's Wing Attack") : A Special Attack featured in Pirate Warriors 4 , Marco in his Human-Beast Form flies circles across the ground, sweeping enemies away with a vortex of flame. In the game's English release, the technique is called Phoenix Raid . Fushicho no Shugeki ( 不死鳥の蹴撃 , Fushichō no Shūgeki , literally meaning "Phoenix's Kicking Attack") : A Special Attack featured in Pirate Warriors 4 , Marco doles out a barrage of consecutive kicks while in his Human-Beast Form, sending enemies in front of him flying back. In the game's English release, the technique is called Phoenix Kick . Fushicho no Kyoshu ( 不死鳥の強襲 , Fushichō no Kyōshū , literally meaning "Phoenix's Assault") : A Special Attack featured in Pirate Warriors 4 , Marco changes into a full phoenix and charges forward while spinning about his own axis, knocking enemies back. In the game's English release, the technique is called Phoenix Assault . Zoan-kei Genjushu ( 「 動物 系」幻獣種 , Zōn-kei Genjūshu , literally meaning "Animal System Mythical Beast Species") : Marco soars into the air, knocking the enemy upwards, then moves to the airborne enemy to kick them down to the ground like he did with Kizaru during the Summit War of Marineford . This attack appears in Burning Blood and in the English version is called Mythical Zoan . Genju Rengeki ( 幻獣連撃 , Genjū Rengeki , literally meaning "Mythical Beast Chain Attack") : Marco flies towards an enemy to dole out rapid consecutive hits in the process of colliding with the enemy. This move appears in Burning Blood and, in the game's English release, is called Beast Chain . The following were techniques of Marco uniquely featured in the discontinued Japanese-only One Piece Thousand Storm mobile game: The following EX-ranked Special Moves: Soen no Kenjo ( 蒼炎の拳浄 , Sōen no Kenjō , lit. "Blue Flame's Purging Fist") Soen no Tenkei ( 蒼炎の天恵 , Sōen no Tenkei , lit. "Blue Flame's Blessing") Soen no Kago ( 蒼炎の加護 , Sōen no Kago , lit. The Shiro Shiro no Mi is a Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to become a living fortress, making the user a Castle Human ( 城人間 , Shiro Ningen , VIZ: "Castle-Man") . It was eaten by Capone Bege . Its powers were first revealed in the Sabaody Archipelago Arc , but the name of the fruit was not given until the Zou Arc . Etymology Shiro ( 城 ) is a Japanese term for "castle". In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, it is called the Castle-Castle Fruit . Strengths The main strength of this fruit is that it allows the user to become a living fortress, the inside of their body holding many openings, including cannon ports, portcullises, and drawbridges; deeper inside contains various rooms, ranging from stone brick rooms for combat to well-furnished meeting halls, complete with long tables, chairs, and even decorations. The user can hold even living people inside their body by shrinking their sizes as long as they are within the vicinity of the user. When the objects and people within the user get out, they return to their normal size. This can be done by exiting a certain distance away from the body, shown by a pinkish layer of an unknown aura centered on the user's location, or through a unique alternative route like the Mirro-World . Once someone enters this aura, they automatically get sucked into the castle. When the user grows to unleash the fortress' full size, the shrinking effect is nulled. In addition, the user's castle defenses can be deployed in a circular formation to give them a full 360 degrees of protection and attack coverage, as well as the potential to conduct decisive surprise ambushes. As long as the user does not manifest castle features on their external body, the castle features of their internal body will remain concealed and unnoticeable. This has the advantage of lulling others into a false sense of security who think they are facing a single individual in combat who appears to be lacking armaments on the surface, only for the fruit's user to reveal the militia and weaponry hidden within. If the user does not wish to engage in combat personally, they can shield themselves by releasing their subordinates and letting them do the fighting for them. The user can also use their body as a storage facility for recreational purposes, as they are shown to house food and drink for subordinates and any outsiders. The user has complete control over the fortress in their body and can telekinetically move around and create objects from inside as they see fit. However, movement from the outside does not seem to affect the inside. The user also has the ability to make a miniature version of themselves through a strange fluid as a way to communicate with those inside; however, the user can still just talk from outside their body in order to be heard. They are capable of being alert and moving around in the real world while also being in their own body. Should any damage occur to the outside body, the miniature version will reflect said damage on itself, feeling pain and showing visible injuries on their equivalent spot of the damaged area. Additionally, the user can also turn their body into an actual fortress, resembling a humanoid golem; while they still retain a humanoid form, they will grow significantly in size as their skin becomes stone and the rooms in the castle become life-sized. The fortress has immense defensive power, being immune to cannon fire and even withstanding a ruthless beating from the Emperor Big Mom (at least for a while), making it a suitable safe haven in the midst of a battlefield. The user is able to turn their body into parts of a fortress, including, for example, tank treads. Weaknesses So far, this Devil Fruit has displayed no direct offensive abilities that affect those outside the user. This Devil Fruit's full potential seems to be dependent on the presence of other people within the user's fortress body in order to use weapons and launch ambushes against threats located outside it. Should the user be inhaling smoke or any similar substances, the insides would be filled with it as well, potentially hindering the user's own subordinates. Anyone can escape his body should they simply exit through an opening, so the user must monitor prisoners at all times or keep the doors closed. The insides of the fortress can also take structural damage such as by catching on fire, although it is unknown how this affects his body. On the outside, when in full fortress form, the castle is a sturdy defense, but not impregnable. Since the fortress is the user's actual body, any damage inflicted onto the walls is effectively injuring the user. Should the giant castle be destroyed, it will disappear and the user will die, and anyone taking refuge within will be expelled into the open. The artillery of the giant fortress seems to have limited ammunition, and they can be clogged by foreign objects, causing them to backfire. Also, the mobile strength of the fortress can be restrained by sufficient strength, such as specialized candy . Other than that, the user is affected by the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Capone Bege primarily uses his ability to launch powerful surprise attacks against unsuspecting adversaries. Holding his entire crew within his own body, they can fire guns or launch cannon blasts from inside, which would return to full size once leaving the boundary of Bege's ability. The troops can also rush outside of their captain's body via horses to overrun and surround their enemies. All of this results in bullets, large cannon volleys, and cavalry seemingly popping out of nowhere from one man. Bege also uses this ability to provide a place for him to have private conversations with other people. When he does this, he is able to appear from a liquid form. When his larger body is not moving, it remains dormant, and his eyes glaze over; however, his body is still capable of walking when he chooses to do so. Having his targets inside his castle grants him a field advantage, allowing him to overwhelm his opponents by forming weapons and traps anywhere he desires. Techniques Castle Tank ( キャッスルタンク , Kyassuru Tanku ) : Bege manifests tank treads around his leg area, allowing him to move at high speed, similar to Franky's technique . It is unknown if his legs are covered by the treads or transformed into them; the anime shows the latter. This was first used to escape Nekomamushi after capturing Sanji and Caesar Clown in his body. Rook In Voller Größe: Big Father ( 城 イン・フォラ・グレーセ 大頭目 , Rūku In Fora Gurēse: Biggu Fāzā , meaning "Castle at Full Size: Big Boss") : By raising his pinky and middle fingers, Bege transforms his entire body into a gigantic, heavily armed, and fortified fortress-like golem, towering over even the massive Big Mom. The golem has a large number of cannons that can fire from various points in its body. The golem has immense defensive power and can easily resist cannon fire and is resilient enough to withstand being smashed apart by an enraged Big Mom, who could easily destroy cities and sink battleships, for a limited period of time. However, it is still Bege's body, and any damage done to it still injures him, and should it be destroyed, Bege himself will die, and anyone within will be thrown out into the open. This was first used to protect all his allied forces after the assassination attempt on Big Mom failed. In the anime, the miniature version of Bege within the castle tended to make large arm motions whenever the castle version made a move. In Voller Größe is German for "In/At full size." This is called Full-Scale Rook!!: The Big Father in the VIZ Manga and Funimation adaptations. Rook: Cannon Preparation ( ルークキャノン プレパレイション , Rūku Kyanon Purepareishon ) : Bege aims the cannons from one of Big Father's shoulders. It is named only in the anime. Cannon Firing ( キャノン ファイアリング , Kyanon Faiaringu ) : Bege shoots with the cannons of Big Father. The cannons can be counter-measured if blocked with something resilient enough, like Katakuri 's mochi or Perospero 's Candy Wall. Named only in the anime. Rook: Movement ( ルーク ムーヴメント , Rūku Mūvumento ) : Big Father's legs are replaced with tank treads, and it moves at greater speed than normal, similar to Castle Tank. It can be used to trample anything in its way, too. Perospero's candy powers were proven effective to block the movements of this technique. Named only in the anime.
