During the Summit

ability_name: One Sword Style of the Flower
ability_description: One Sword Style of the Flower is a swordsmanship style developed by Hyougoro . Overview One Sword Style of the Flower is a style of single wielding swordsmanship invented by Hyougoro. Little is known about the style, but the only known attack focuses on speed. Techniques Dohatsu Kohai ( 怒髪光拝 , Dohatsu Kōhai , lit. "Infuriated Light Worship") : Hyougoro swings his sword slowly and charges at full speed to slash through multiple enemies while leaving some kind of fire generated by unknown means behind. The name of this attack alludes to Hyougoro's flame-like hair; the word dohatsu ( 怒髪 ) means "infuriated", but the individual kanji meanings are "angry" and "hair". In the VIZ Manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Flaming Hair of Holy Rage .

ability_name: Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Seiryu
ability_description: The Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Seiryu is a Mythical Zoan -type Devil Fruit that allows its user to transform into a hybrid and full Azure Dragon at will. Belonging to the "strongest class of Devil Fruit", it was once a World Noble treasure before it was stolen by Charlotte Linlin and eaten by Kaidou during the God Valley Incident 38 years ago. At some point, Dr. Vegapunk extracted a sample from a captive Kaidou's Lineage Factor and, with it, successfully created an artificial replica of this Devil Fruit . However, the given transformation was not of the same color as the original, and so solely for this reason the result was deemed a failure by Vegapunk, accidentally tricking the World Government into thinking the fruit was powerless. The replica was eventually consumed by Kouzuki Momonosuke , causing him to obtain a dragon transformation nigh-identical to that of Kaidou, save for its color. Etymology Uo ( 魚 ) is Japanese for "fish". Seiryū ( 青龍 ) is the Japanese name of the Azure Dragon. Appearance The fruit is spherical and slightly oblong in shape, somewhat resembling a pinecone. Its blue exterior gives the appearance of having overlapping scales, similar to those covering a dragon's skin: each "scale" has a small, dark-blue oval in the middle that bears a signature Devil Fruit spiral at the bottom, while the scales' outer portions are of a lighter-blue shade. On its top, the fruit possesses two identical, separate stems, with one being purple and the other a lighter pink shade, reminiscent of the horns of the same mythological creature, which attach to a tuft of orange leaves. The artificial version of this fruit takes the form of a blue apple with double ring patterns throughout the fruit, the outer rings being darker green than the inner ones' lighter. Transformations This fruit's ability user can turn into a giant, serpentine, blue-scaled dragon called Azure Dragon : a type of Eastern dragon ( 龍 , Ryū ) . Additionally, as with all Zoans, they can access a Human-Beast Form, becoming a smaller half-dragon humanoid. Apart from scales, acquired dragon features include horns (a second pair in Kaidou's case), sharp fangs, and limbs with clawed hands and feet. In his full Beast Form, Kaidou grows to rival a small town in size, making this one of the largest yet-seen Zoan transformations. His Human-Beast Form, while not as colossal, is still very large and, in shape, comes closer to his Human Form, with scales on his arms, outer torso, back, and legs as well as six horns on his head, claws on the hands and feet, and a long tail. In addition to his standard Human-Beast Form, Kaidou has on several occasions showcased an alternate hybrid form, specifically when under the influence of Shuron Hakke . Such form appears slightly bulkier and has different facial features: a full set of fangs, a wider nose, and eyebrows similar to his full Beast form. In the anime, his scales also have a darker, more vibrant blue color (tinged purple) compared to Kaidou's other forms. The artificial duplicate of this fruit grants whoever eats it a similar transformation, with certain minor differences, the main one being the color of the scales being pink rather than azure. Also, the user's natural size affects the transformation's size proportionally, as Momonosuke at 8 years old assumed a comparatively small form, but once he was aged to 28 , he became as big as Kaidou as a dragon. So far, the Human-Beast Form for the artificial version has yet to be seen. Unlike the natural version, the artificial version does not seem to affect the user's clothes. Gallery Beast Form. Human-Beast Form. Human-Beast Form (Shuron Hakke). Beast Form (Shuron Hakke). Artificial Fruit Beast Form, as a child. Beast Form, as an adult. Strengths As a Mythical Zoan, regarded by some as the strongest Devil Fruit type, the giant dragon transformation comes with a massive boost to the user's physical abilities, far greater than what is seen with most other Zoan types. The full dragon form's strength befits its size, as the user can severely damage structures like houses merely by brushing by them. When Kaidou flew around Okobore Town in a drunken stupor, it was believed that if he continued wreaking havoc senselessly, not just the town but Kuri itself would be wiped out. The user's arms, while thinner compared to the rest of their dragon body, are still very strong, a single swipe of which can send large fish-men flying through rock formations with great force and rip apart houses with ease, even when drunk. In the anime, the full dragon form's power is further exemplified, with Kaidou seen causing intense, gusty shockwaves simply by moving through the air or being thrown around, as well as with a mere puff or burp. He was able to send Monkey D. Luffy flying with immense force using a tail swipe and easily overpowered Luffy's Haki -imbued use of Gear 3 with one of his dragon hands, as well as by thrusting his head forward. Two decades ago, in his fight with Kouzuki Oden , Kaidou in his Beast Form repeatedly clashed with the samurai on even terms, including matching the force of Oden's Tougen Shirataki with his head, countering his blades with his claws and fangs, and even using one of his mustache tendrils to ensnare and toss Oden away. Apart from raw strength, the user's size as a dragon belies their mobility, as they can move about at great speeds while keeping pace with significantly fast, human-sized foes. The scales covering the user's dragon body are noted to be extraordinarily durable. Blows of incredible power, including ones strengthened by regular Armament Haki, have proven utterly ineffective at hurting Kaidou—e.g., when in full dragon form, he shrugged off a nonstop pummeling by Luffy's strongest Gear 3 attacks, without being scathed in the slightest. Nonetheless, as with Kaidou's extremely tough human skin, his dragon scales can be penetrated, and blood drawn from him, using attacks that bypass external defenses, like advanced-grade Armament Haki . Beside the transformation itself, the fruit bestows multiple supernatural abilities, the most out of any Mythical Zoan seen so far. The most prominent of these is the power to conjure Flame Clouds ( 焔雲 , Homura-gumo ) , with which the user can travel through the air by using them as footholds, effectively enabling flight; Kaidou's dragon arms tend to be continuously encircled by fiery rings, which may be said clouds helping him fly more effectively through the air. Also, the Flame Clouds can be used to lift other objects, including entire islands , into the air and move them, for extended periods even and while using their other powers simultaneously. Furthermore, there is no apparent need for the user to transform in order to manifest (or at least maintain) Flame Clouds. In addition, the user can create and control various elements as a dragon, both in their full Beast and Human-Beast Form. The most commonly seen element used is fire, such as a concentrated scorching blast of fire that the user breathes out, which is powerful enough to burn entire armies to death as Kaido did against the Kozuki rebel army. The fire breath has immense range, travel many kilometers in the span of seconds and instantly decimate a large, earthen structure , as well as rapidly shooting fireballs in the anime. Aside from flames, the user can launch lightning bolts while roaring (Kaidou is able to summon lightning bolts from the sky without transforming as seen in the anime) and cutting blades of wind while blowing, on top of stirring up strong, fast-rotating tornados. It is also shown that Kaidou can summon large hails of ice in One Piece: Pirate Warriors 4 . The user's Human-Beast Form sees their draconic strength focused in significantly smaller frame, with this diminished size granting them a boost in agility and speed while seeming overall better adapted to close combat. The user also retains access to their elemental attacks, like their fire breath (though with a smaller range in accordance with their smaller body size, compared to their full dragon form). Weaknesses Due to the massive amount of space the user takes up after entering their full Beast Form, their ability to move about in enclosed spaces is greatly hindered. Additionally, depending on how much care is given to their surroundings, the user's transformed body having a propensity for collateral damage may become a liability for allies. The user's massive body also leaves more openings, making them an easier target for attacks, especially multi-directional assault from more than one enemy. While the user's dragon scales are immensely tough, the user remains vulnerable to attacks that, by their nature, can bypass external defenses. Usage of advanced, emitted Armament Haki , in particular, can let opposing parties hurt the user's dragon form (even if used by enemies that are physically far weaker than the user). Other examples of effective attacks reaching the target's insides are ones utilizing powerful soundwaves or the Ope Ope no Mi 's "surgical" techniques. Alternatively, the sheer power of Supreme King-infused attacks has proven great enough to surpass their resilience. Moreover, the user's signature ability to breathe fire can be countered by an opponent who knows how to cut flames . The Flame Clouds generated by the user will also weaken as the user is pushed to their physical limit (and can also be extinguished by water, like normal fire). If the user loses consciousness, their Flame Clouds will disappear completely. Besides all these factors, the user is affected by the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage After extracting Kaidou 's Lineage Factor , Vegapunk attempted to create his own Artificial Devil Fruit , something that he succeeded in, but he then deemed the project a failure. Despite all this, having consumed it, Kouzuki Momonosuke has shown certain abilities no different from Kaidou such as enhanced strength, breathing flames and conjuring clouds, more so after being matured by Shinobu 's Juku Juku no Mi , though he has yet to master them. Vegapunk revealed that he deemed this Artificial Devil Fruit a failure simply because the user turned into a Pink Dragon, instead of an Azure Dragon. Kaidou uses this fruit's power frequently in combat. He typically starts out battles in his full dragon form and transforms back into a human upon getting more serious, his base form seeming more suited for intense and up-close, one-on-one combat. If his adversaries prove strong enough to handle both his human and full dragon forms, Kaidou will enter his Human-Beast Form—quite possibly the strongest of his three basic forms, it is generally used in all out fights against stronger and faster enemies that his two other forms have trouble dealing with. When engaging foes in his full dragon form, Kaidou frequently hovers a considerable distance away from them, out of melee range, so as to assault them with destructive, long-ranged attacks of various elements, like his fire breath. In actual close combat, he has been seen chomping down on opponents within reach or powerfully swiping at them with his claws. When entering his Human-Beast Form, Kaidou takes to wielding his kanabo, Hassaikai , like he does in his Human Form. With the boost he gains in strength, he can swing the club with absolute destructive force, causing massive shockwaves and striking melee blows too dangerous for most enemies to take head on. Becoming far more agile than as a full dragon, Kaidou can move around by slithering through the air, using his dragon tail to propel himself forward or upwards, which aids his offensive moves. Moreover, his tail is prehensile and capable of capturing opponents off guard. Kaidou also demonstrates the ability to breath fire in his hybrid form, though with less force due to his decrease in size. Once Kaidou acknowledges an opponent and gets drunk enough, he is able to engage in Shuron Hakke , a unique fighting style that takes advantage of Kaidou's drunken mood swings and allows him to quickly switch between his Zoan forms and employ more unpredictable techniques. During certain moods, Kaidou may even shift into completely unique forms outside his usual three. For example, while in a laughing or bloodthirsty mood, Kaidou manifests a bulkier version of his Human-Beast Form that can use stronger versions of his kanabo attacks, and while in a "thieving" mood, he turns into a smaller, slightly more humanoid variant of his Full Beast form that can swiftly fly around in sharp curves to dodge overwhelmingly fast enemy attacks. Both in and outside of battle, Kaidou can create his "Flame Clouds" to use primarily as footholds or to levitate himself in order to take flight; he can thus easily travel between islands by flying quickly and crossing the open sea, such as between Onigashima and the Wano mainland. He can also levitate objects, even gigantic ones, by creating enough of his clouds to surround whatever he wishes to float away. This was chiefly seen when Kaidou sent the entire island of Onigashima aloft to proceed with his New Onigashima Project , levitating the island toward Wano's Flower Capital . Kaidou Bolo Breath ( 熱息 , Boro Buresu , literally meaning "Heat Breath") : Kaidou's signature attack. He gathers fire inside his mouth before "breathing" it out into a huge, conical blast toward his target of choice. This blast travels rapidly (in the anime covering a tremendous distance within moments) and is extremely powerful, being able to burn entire armies to death. It can instantly decimate a large structure (like a castle's ruins ) while leaving the surrounding terrain (like a mountaintop) blown to bits and scorched, can set a sizeable portion of a forest ablaze, and even be split into numerous smaller fireballs shot out like bullets. Despite this move's strength and scope, it can be countered by Foxfire Style . When Raizo cast this attack back at Kaidou via his scrolls , it proved able to somewhat damage the latter's own dragon body. Bolo is a term used in Central America and Mexico meaning "drunk". In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Blast Breath . When Kaidou used this technique for the first time during the God Valley Incident , he simply called it "Ryu" ( 龍 , Ryū , VIZ: "Dragon") , stretching it out for emphasis. In the anime, Kaidou is shown to be able to diffuse this attack into multiple smaller shots. This spreads out the damage and makes it harder to dodge, but also makes it significantly weaker than the original form. He used it against the Nine Red Scabbards in an effort to make them keep their distance. Kaifu ( 壊風 , Kaifū , literally meaning "Demolishing Wind") : An attack where Kaidou blows strongly, thereby creating powerful, crescent wind blades that can easily cut through solid stone as well as human bodies. The move was first seen being used in Kaidou's fight against the Nine Red Scabbards on the roof of Onigashima's Skull Dome , severing Kikunojo 's left arm, but was not named until Kaidou used it against the Worst Generation members on the same roof. Denjiro described the wind blades as kamaitachi ( かまいたち ) , a Japanese term for cutting whirlwind derived from the yōkai Kamaitachi . In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the attack is called Demolition Gust . Kaidou is also capable of performing this move while in Human-Beast form. In this case, he quickly swings his Hassaikai to generate a vertical crescent-shaped wind blade that travels straight forward, akin to a Tobu Zangeki . Tatsumaki ( 龍巻 , Tatsumaki , literally meaning "Dragon Twister") : Kaidou spins his serpentine body into a coil to summon several tornadoes around him. Because of their wind speed, these tornadoes can pull people away from the ground and within Kaidou's range in midair, where he may chomp down on them. The technique was first seen in use against the Worst Generation members on the Skull Dome's roof. It was effective in capturing a defenseless Monkey D. Luffy , but Roronoa Zoro was able to neutralize it with his own Kokujo: O Tatsumaki technique. The name of the attack is the Japanese word for "tornado" ( 竜巻 , tatsumaki ) , but with the kyūjitai of the "dragon" ( 龍 ) kanji instead of the shinjitai ( 竜 ) , and thus is not to be confused with the Santoryu technique of a similar name. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Dragon Twister . Tatsumaki Kaifu ( 龍巻壊風 , Tatsumaki Kaifū , literally meaning "Dragon Twister Demolishing Wind") : Kaidou spins the lower portion of his elongated dragon body at great speeds, in a manner resembling a tornado himself, that helps to gather enough wind to release and direct the crescent wind blades of Kaifu towards his targets. This move was first seen being used in Kaidou's fight against the Worst Generation. In Episode 1018 , the technique is depicted differently, with Kaidou spinning his lower body in a tornado-like fashion but releasing Kaifu from his mouth as usual. This was later changed to its proper execution in Episode 1064 . In the VIZ manga and Funimation subs, the attack is called Dragon Twister Demolition Breath . In the Funimation dub, this attack is called Dragon Twister Demolition Gust . Kaen Daiko ( 火龍大炬 , Kaen Daiko , literally meaning "Fire Dragon Great Torch") : Kaidou breathes a large amount of flames from his mouth, which coat his original dragon body and take its shape. The fire dragon dwarfs Kaidou's already large dragon form and rivals Onigashima in size. It produces enough heat to near-instantly melt Onigashima's remaining "horn" by lightly brushing against it, preventing opponents from touching Kaidou, though Luffy, a user of advanced Armament Haki , is able to counter this due to not having to make physical contact. The name of the technique is a reference to the rakugo story Kaen Daiko ( 火焔太鼓 , "Flaming Drum") . In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Flame Dragon Torch . Shoryu: Kaen Hakke ( 昇龍 火焔八卦 , Shōryū: Kaen Hakke , literally meaning "Rising Dragon: Flame/Blaze Eight Trigrams") : Kaidou lunges at his opponent in his Kaen Daiko state, and unleashes an explosive bite with his flaming jaws. This technique was first used to counter Luffy's Gomu Gomu no Bajrang Gun . In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, this is called Rising Dragon: Flame Bagua . Momonosuke Bolo Breath ( 熱息 , Boro Buresu , literally meaning "Heat Breath") : Like Kaidou, Momonosuke gathers a large amount of fire inside his mouth before "breathing" it out into a huge, conical blast toward his target of choice. It was first used against Aramaki 's wooden form , burning him to a crisp and forcing him to regenerate from a sprout, although Momonosuke initially struggled to replicate Kaidou's technique. History 38 years ago, the fruit was once property of the World Nobles and, alongside the Nikyu Nikyu no Mi , one of the prizes in the Native Hunting Competition on God Valley . Ginny considered them to be crucial to escaping the island and formed a plan to steal them. After a subsequent clash between the Rocks Pirates , Roger Pirates , and Marines , Emporio Ivankov and Bartholomew Kuma managed to steal the Devil Fruits. Ivankov prepared to eat the fruit while Kuma ate the Nikyu Nikyu no Mi, but then Charlotte Linlin assaulted Ivankov and snatched the fruit from him. She planned to give the fruit to her son Charlotte Perospero , but Kaidou struck her and stole the fruit for himself, eating it immediately. Because of this, Linlin considered him indebted to her for life. Sometime after eating the fruit, Kaidou came into Marine captivity, and the scientist Vegapunk was able to extract his Lineage Factor for the purpose of creating an Artificial Devil Fruit that would grant the same exact abilities. Despite the success in that area, however, Vegapunk deemed it a failure because he couldn't get the dragon's color right, and the fruit was stored away on Punk Hazard . 34 years later, it was eaten by Kouzuki Momonosuke , thus granting him a dragon transformation much like Kaidou's, though Momonosuke's power was initially out of his control.

After awakening his genetic enhancements, Sanji's speed and agility increased tremendously, enabling him to casually move so fast that his opponent Queen first mistook his swiftness for invisibility. ability_name: Ryu Ryu no Mi, Model: Pteranodon
ability_description: For other models of this fruit, see Ryu Ryu no Mi (Disambiguation) . The Ryu Ryu no Mi, Model: Pteranodon is an Ancient Zoan -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to transform into a pteranodon hybrid and a full pteranodon at will. It was eaten by King . Etymology Ryū ( 竜 ) is Japanese for "dragon" and contained in the Japanese word for "pterosaur", yokuryū ( 翼竜 ) . Strengths and Weaknesses A user of this fruit's power can turn into a pteranodon: a large, flight-capable, predatory reptile from prehistoric times. Once transformed, they become able to fly at tremendous speed, covering large distances rapidly, and enjoy a significant boost to their overall physical abilities, as typical for a carnivorous Ancient Zoan user. Additionally, the full Beast Form as displayed by King is notably large, several times bigger than an average-sized human (though it is worth noting that King is already much larger than normal people in his normal form). Like other Ryu Ryu no Mi models, the pteranodon Zoan grants the user a tough, dragon-esque hide that significantly increases their durability to a level above regular Zoans. As for offense, the great (flying) speed, strength, and size conferred by the transformation allows the user to violently push back and cause notable damage to whatever they fly into headfirst, be it a person or massive structure (e.g., a large ship ). The user's pteranodon beak is long, sturdy, and an effective pointed weapon, allowing King to affix himself to smaller targets and then divebomb into structures in order to tear them apart. Moreover, the user is given the supposedly natural ability of using their pteranodon head like a slingshot of sorts: pulling their beak back into their face by pulling on their head crest, then releasing their grip to shoot out their head like a bullet, piercing whatever they aim at with incredible force. According to King, this conforms with how pteranodons used to hunt their prey in ancient times. Aside from their head and beak, the user's wings can make potent weapons as well given their sturdiness, able to generate cutting winds or clash against swords as if they were blades themselves. No weaknesses are known of this ability beside those that are standard for Devil Fruits. Usage King uses his ability primarily for physical—especially aerial—combat, with his ability to fly being highly advantageous for travel purposes, especially over the sea. King has proven able to soar at tremendous speeds, in the anime overtaking Sanji in a high-speed aerial chase while the latter was donning his Raid Suit . In his full pteranodon form, King can fly into objects with immense speed and force, potentially damaging them greatly in the process. When flying into the Queen Mama Chanter , such a midair collision (with a flaming kick in the anime) knocked the enormous ship back with enough force to throw off Big Mom herself. Later, King presented a danger to a Raid Suit-wearing Sanji by diving into him with his strong, pointed beak, challenging said suit's defenses to the point that King claimed he would split his foe in half upon impact (something he ultimately failed at due to the suit proving too durable). In addition to this, King's head too can function as a weapon: pulling back at his head crest allows King to shoot out his head like an extremely fast, oversized bullet, which King has noted is how the pteranodons of the ancient past used to hunt their prey. When he is in his hybrid form, King can use his pteranodon wing-arms like swords, allowing him to release powerful air blades by swinging them. These slash attacks can deal massive damage to whatever they hit, and he can launch them in rapid succession. In general, King's wings are shown to be strong enough to clash with and even overpower Famous Blades being wielded by a master swordsman , and through built-up momentum via flight, the All-Star can use said appendages to push foes away with great force. Moreover, for defense, King can use his durable wings as a shield; doing so, he was able to withstand staying amid a powerful whirlwind of slashes without receiving any scratches. As a lunarian , King can also combine his transformation with his ability to emit fire ; he has been seen attacking with barrages of flame bullets shot from his wings, almost resembling a living fighter aircraft in the process. Techniques Tankyudon ( 丹弓皇 , Tankyūdon , meaning "Vermilion Bow Emperor") : A melee attack where King in his Hybrid Form (with the flame on his back out, hence increasing his speed) flies toward his opponent at a low altitude, using the gained momentum to hit said opponent with a sweeping strike of his pteranodon wing. When used against Roronoa Zoro , the attack overpowered the latter's Ul-Tora Gari technique before blowing the swordsman back with tremendous force, making him bust right through the Skull Dome 's outer wall. The name of the attack may be a pun on " tanuki udon " ( たぬきうどん , tanuki udon ) as well as the word tankyū ( 短弓 ) , referring to Japanese shortbows. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Imperial Archer . Tempuraudon ( 貂自尊皇 , Tenpuraudon , meaning "Proud Marten Emperor") : A melee attack used by King in his full pteranodon form (while the flame on his back is out, hence increasing his attacking speed; the first time the attack was seen in the anime, however, the flame was on). King pulls back on his head crest with one arm, stretching his face flat and causing his beak to retract into his face. He then lets go of the crest so that his head flings forward at extreme speed like a slingshot (similar to Luffy 's Gomu Gomu No Kane ), straight forward over a long distance in order to strike his opponent with massive force, using his beak to pierce them. When King first performed this attack against Zoro, the swordsman was barely able to dodge the attack, calling it unblockable and like a "laser." The second time King used Tempuraudon against Zoro, the move destroyed significant parts of Onigashima 's shoreline rocks. The name of the attack is a pun on " tempura udon" ( 天ぷらうどん , tenpura udon ) and contains puraudo ( プラウド ) , the Japanese pronunciation of "proud" in English. In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Imperial Deep Pride Stake , as a pun on "deep-fried steak" (tempura being itself a deep-fried dish). Barizodon ( 刃裏双皇 , Barizōdon , meaning "Blade Back Twin Emperor") : An attack where King, while in flight, employs both his pteranodon wings akin to blades, using their forward-most edge to launch a series of destructive, elliptic air (or possibly flame) projectiles at an opponent. The attack was first seen in use against Zoro but was mostly blocked by the latter's sword. The technique's name is a pun on barizōgon ( 罵詈雑言 ) , a four-character idiom meaning "vilification/vicious insults." In the VIZ manga and Funimation adaptations, the technique is called Imperial Slings And Arrows . Video Game Only Techniques Yokuryu no Entotsu ( 翼 竜 の 炎 突 , Yokuryū no Entotsu , meaning "Pterosaur's Flame Thrust") : A basic attack used by King in the game One Piece Thousand Storm . He, as a pteranodon, flies into the enemy beak-first and covered in flames, dealing medium-level fire damage. Yokuryu no Shuen ( 翼 竜 の 蹴 炎 , Yokuryū no Shūen , meaning "Pterosaur's Kicking Flame") : A special attack used by King in the game Thousand Storm . He jumps up into the air as he transforms into a pteranodon, then descends onto the enemy to strike down on them with a flaming kick, dealing extra-heavy fire damage. Yokuryu no Enbu ( 翼 竜 の 炎 舞 , Yokuryū no Enbu , meaning "Pterosaur's Flame Dance") : A special attack used by King in the game Thousand Storm . Transformed into a pteranodon, he flies in circles over the enemy to engulf them in a swirling, then violently exploding inferno on the ground, dealing extra-heavy fire damage. Kien Banjo ( 斬 炎 万 畳 , Kien Banjō , meaning "Cutting Flame, Ten Thousand Foldings") : A special attack used by King in Thousand Storm . He, while in his pteranodon form, rapidly waves his flame-covered wings to deal repeated, superfast slashing attacks that deal extra-heavy fire damage. The attack's name is a pun on kienbanjō ( 気炎万丈 ) , a four-character idiom meaning "in high spirits/talking big." Amakake Ryudon ( 天 翔 龍 皇 , Amakake Ryūdon , meaning "Soaring Dragon Emperor") : A special attack featured in Thousand Storm . King jumps up into the air, then in his pteranodon form descends onto the enemy beak-first to pierce them, while enveloped in flames to deal extra-heavy fire damage. The attack's name is likely a pun on "ankake udon" ( あんかけうどん ) , an udon dish where the soup is thickened with powdered kudzu.

The Kyara Kyara no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that provides consciousness to non-living souls, as well as allowing the soul to merge with others. It was eaten by Lip Shark . Etymology "Kyara" is derived from kyarakutā ( キャラクター ) , the Japanese way of pronouncing the English word "character". Appearance The Kyara Kyara no Mi resembles purple poop, drawn in a manga-esque style. Like all Devil Fruits, it has a pattern of swirls on it, however, it lacks any stem. Strengths and Weaknesses The main strength of this Devil Fruit is the ability to merge with other people, providing massive increases in strength and speed. It is unclear if there are restrictions on who the user can merge with. After the timeskip , Lip Shark could merge with the bow of the Maying Gorry . Aside from the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses, there are no known weaknesses; Lip Shark states that he draws power from Fischer's millions of fans, allowing for the amount of power increase, but it's unclear if this a weakness of himself or the Devil Fruit. Usage Lip Shark uses its abilities to powerup the Fischer's Pirates members, allowing them to defeat much stronger opponents. Techniques Transform ( 変身 , Henshin ) : Lip Shark merges into another person, giving them a large physical boost. An ability based on their personality is manifested as well. When this ability was first used, it was called Union ( 合体 , Gattai ) . Two-Headed Mascot Mordo ( 2頭身マスコット モードウオ , 2 Tōshin Masukotto Mōdouo ) : Lip Shark transforms himself into a young boy, allowing him to interact with the environment as a human would. Transform used with Silk Road. Lip Shark in his Two-Headed Mascot Mordo form.

Notably, a burst of 's Haki being released was potent enough to force the , who are themselves powerful Haki users, out of their Awakened Zoan transformations. This application has only been displayed by individuals whose Despite its great capabilities, the Haki in a person's body is not limitless and it can be depleted from overuse, rendering the user unable to use it for a set period of time while it regenerates.
Haki As a former Fleet Admiral and the leader of the Marines, Sengoku is fully capable of using Haki. He is also one of the very few people in the world who can use all three types of Haki.

Even after getting overwhelmed and Kuzan going for a killer blow, Garp still survived. Garp is also extremely fast and agile, seemingly disappearing as soon as he starts moving. The Nuke Nuke no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to phase through solid objects. It was eaten by Tanaka . Etymology Nukeru ( 抜ける ) means, among other things, "to go/pass through". The Funimation adaptations call it the Through-Through Fruit , a reference to how the user slips through solid objects. In One Piece Treasure Cruise , it is called the Phase-Phase Fruit . Strengths and Weaknesses The main strength of this fruit's power is to allow the user to phase through solid objects, allowing for an easier and more unconventional way to get around, as well as avoiding attacks from non-organic weapons and projectiles. The user can also share their intangibility with whatever they touch, allowing people or objects to phase through solid matter along with him, or making the ground intangible to make people fall through the floor. A weakness of this fruit is that the user cannot phase through living tissue, as seen when Tanaka could not escape from Robin 's grasp or avoid Sanji 's kicks . Other than that, the user is also affected by the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Due to this, the user apparently cannot phase through seawater. Usage As a security guard, Tanaka can use his fruit to easily travel to anywhere in Gran Tesoro where there may be a disturbance. Several rooms in Gran Tesoro do not have doors, allowing them to be accessible only by people whom Tanaka takes into the room. Tanaka sometimes uses his power against criminals by opening up the ground under them and sending them into a large cavern. In combat, Tanaka jumps in and out of the ground around his opponents, shooting at them with his gun when he pops up and emerging in different places in order to disorient them. Techniques Through Illusion ( スルー・イリュージョン , Surū Iryūjon ) : Tanaka's signature move, in which he jumps in and out of the ground repeatedly, phasing into it and escaping detection. He emerges in various places around his opponents in order to overwhelm them, and shoots at them with his gun while in the air. Bottomless Hell ( ボトムレス・ヘル , Botomuresu Heru ) : Tanaka creates a giant hole underneath his opponents, sending them falling all the way to the Golden Prison on Gran Tesoro.

Main Geppo Tekkai Rankyaku Soru Shigan Kami-e Rokuogan For the chapter of the same name, see Chapter 347 . The Six Powers are a set of superhuman martial arts techniques. While accessible to any combatant, they are predominantly used by World Government forces, in particular Cipher Pol , with CP9 demanding proficiency in all six aspects. Overview The Six Powers, in essence, take simple combat maneuvers and advance their capabilities to superhuman levels using prodigious speed and strength. There are two movement-focused techniques (the superhuman speed of Soru and the aerial movement of Geppo ), two offensive techniques ( Shigan , where a finger becomes as powerful as a gun, and Rankyaku , where a leg becomes as sharp as a blade), and two defensive techniques (the enhanced durability of Tekkai and the unparalleled slipperiness of Kami-e ). According to Rob Lucci , those who master the Six Powers will have the strength of a hundred men. The overall strength of a user is measured by Doriki , which Fukurou can measure by using his "Te-awase." The higher the level, the stronger each technique's potency becomes. It is common for users to specialize in a particular technique, such as Fukurou with Soru, Jabra with Tekkai, Kaku with Rankyaku, or Lucci and Who's-Who with Shigan. By fully mastering all Six Powers, one becomes capable of utilizing a seventh technique known as Rokuogan , a devastatingly powerful offensive technique capable of inflicting internal injuries without making physical contact with the target. This is an extremely rare power, with Lucci currently being its only known user. The Six Powers are the main combat techniques for the World Government 's Cipher Pol agents, in particular, CP9 and CP0 . Some of the Marines have demonstrated its usage as well; Koby used Soru against Monkey D. Luffy , Vice Admirals Momonga and Dalmatian used it to confront Luffy at Marineford, Momonga also used Geppo against Luffy, and Dalmatian also used Shigan against Luffy, Vice Admiral Vergo used it on several G-5 marines on Punk Hazard , and Captain Tashigi used it during her confrontation against Monet . The (non-canon) former Marines Z and Binz are Six Powers users, with Z being able to master it and Binz seen using at least Soru. It is unknown exactly how much the Six Powers are spread around the Marines and how widely known the techniques are, but it seems the most commonly known technique is Soru. Outside of the Marines, there are also others who learned the Six Powers techniques, or adapt their principles to create their own, similar techniques. Monkey D. Luffy utilized his Gear 2 mode to grasp the concepts of Soru, gaining an advanced version of it, while Sanji gained an advanced variant of Geppo that he can utilize both in midair and underwater. It should be noted, though, that full mastery of a Six Powers technique requires one's mind to keep up with their body in order to properly execute the maneuver, meaning rudimentary approximations of these techniques may be less effective due to being improperly controlled. Users See also the associated category: Six Powers Users . Particularly strong concentrations of Armament Haki in a body part will cause the edges of the coating to take the shape of flames. Luffy, , and Katakuri have been shown with flame-shaped Hardening; the necessary process to attain this is currently unknown. Notably, while this shape has only been shown appearing for Luffy and Katakuri when they are performing their strongest attacks, Big Mom generated a flame-shaped coating while casually blocking an attack from Luffy. Although hardening is extremely tough, it is not impenetrable, as strong attacks using weaker Armament Haki or even none at all have proven to be able to overwhelm hardening defenses.

Three Sword Style Two Sword Style One Sword Style No Sword Style Nine Sword Style Team Combinations Anime Only Video Game Only "Two Sword Style" redirects here. This is about Zoro's Two Sword Style techniques. For Kouzuki Oden 's sword style, see Oden Two Sword Style . Two Sword Style is another related sword/blade-style that Roronoa Zoro developed over the course of his journey, when in situations where his techniques have had to adapt for one reason or another. Techniques These are the attacks he uses with two swords. Two Sword Style was also Zoro's original fighting style when he began to learn how to wield a sword, before accepting Kuina's sword and practicing Three Sword Style. Before the Timeskip Taka Nami ( 鷹波 , Taka Nami , literally meaning "Hawk Wave") : While airborne, creates a powerful gust of wind to knock opponents over. It was first used to wipe out some Baroque Works agents at Whisky Peak . It can be done while standing on the ground. Zoro says this attack is very useful against a group of weak enemies. The pun here is Takanami can mean "high waves" in Japanese. This attack was the first non-Three Sword Style technique Zoro uses in the series. This is called Hawk Wave in the English adaptations. In One Piece: Grand Battle! Swan Colosseum , the attack used as Zoro's midair down attack involves Zoro first diving at a diagonal angle with one of his swords pointed, and upon landing he rolls along the ground bracing himself with his swords before waving them out for a very short-ranged wind blast around himself. During the raid on Onigashima, Zoro used an enhanced version of this technique against some of the Beasts Pirates who were guarding the gates, where he swings Sandai Kitetsu and Enma to create a purple and red hawk-shaped projectile. Sai Kuru ( 犀回 , Sai Kuru , literally meaning "Rhino Revolve") : Zoro holds his swords in front of him pointing up, like rhinoceros' horns, and spin-slashes around himself in an instant (almost akin to Tatsu Maki , but more briefly). This was first seen being used against the Franky Family when they "ambushed" Zoro on the Going Merry . Pun with the Japanese pronunciation of the English word "cycle". This is called Rhino Cycle in the English adaptations and Rhino Rampage in the localization of One Piece: Grand Battle! Rush! (dubbed by 4Kids). Nitoryu Iai: Rashomon ( 二刀流 ｢居合｣ 羅生門 , Nitōryū Iai: Rashōmon , literally meaning "Two Sword/Blade Style Acting-Draw: Rashomon/Gauze Life Gate") : A dual sword drawing technique so powerful that it can split large obstacles in half. Zoro draws his swords, and sheathes them quickly, able to split two train carriages in half. Built in the year 789, the Rashōmon was arguably the grandest and most famous gate in Kyōto. The name of this move probably comes from the double-gate/double-sword connotation, as well as being the 'grandest move' in his Two Sword Style lineup. This was first seen being used to cut open two sea train carriages filled with Marines and World Government agents (none of the passengers were harmed at all). This is called Two-Sword Style Castle Gate in the VIZ manga, Two-Sword Style Sword Draw Rashomon in the Funimation adaptations, and Castle Gate in the English localization of One Piece: Pirate Warriors 3 . It is also used as a counter/reversal in Jump Ultimate Stars as opposed to a direct attack. Nanajuni Pound Ho ( 七十二 煩悩 鳳 , Nanajūni Pondo Hō , kanji meaning "Seventy-Two Klesha Phoenix"; furigana meaning "72 Pound Phoenix/Cannon") : Same principle as the Sanjuroku Pound Ho . Holding his two swords horizontally above the shoulder, Zoro then performs a circular swing that launches two air compressed projectiles spiraling towards the target instead of one, making it twice as powerful. This was first seen being used against Kaku to counter his Rankyaku "Hakurai" technique. This is called 72 Pound Phoenix in the VIZ manga and 72 Caliber Phoenix in the Funimation dub. The attack's name is actually a very heavy pun, it is written out as "Phoenix of the 72 Kleshas" in the manga with a skewed reading attached that makes it "72 Pound Ho" when read out: Ho means both "cannon" and "phoenix"; however, the attached kanji is for "phoenix", making that the literal translation and the "cannon" reading a pun on that, both of which are correct. The "pound" part is a skewed reading of the kanji bonnou for "klesha", a Buddhist term for "worldly desires of evil thoughts", and is a reference to the weight of the largest solid iron sphere that would fit down a cannon's barrel (a 72-pound cannon would fire 72-pound round shot). In Pirate Warriors 3 , when used as the second input of his inverted C3 chain (Trianglex2, Squarex2), Zoro instead runs with both swords at his left side for a rushing double-swing to his right, though no projectiles emerge whatsoever (and the final input leads into his 1080 Pound Phoenix). Nigiri ( 弐斬り , Nigiri , literally meaning "Two Cutter") : A stance where two swords are held parallel so that the tips are pointing to his right or left side, this move is like a pre-requirement to perform: Toro , Otoro , Hirameki, Samon , and Maguma . This was first seen being used against Kaku. The pun in this is taken from sushi: the "Nigiri" in Nigirizushi roughly means "hand-rolled." Toro ( 登楼 , Tōrō , literally meaning "Climbing Tower") : Two air-based projectile slashes are sent upwards while jumping as Zoro swings his swords in an upward motion. This was first seen being used against Kaku . The pun in this is that "Toro" is an expensive cut of tuna for sushi; a fattier cut than the normal, while the technique's kanji is an actual term for climbing a tower (or to "visit a brothel"); another difference between the tuna "toro" has no extended vowels, while the tower-term "tourou" has two extended "u" vowels when written in romaji. In the English adaptations, this is called Tower Climb . Otoro ( 応登楼 , Ōtōrō , literally meaning "Reply Climbing Tower") : Two air-based projectile slashes are sent downwards while falling as Zoro swings his swords downwards, with the gravity complimenting the force of the attack. This was first seen being used against Kaku. The pun in this is that "Ootoro" is an even more expensive cut of tuna for sushi; the underbelly which is said to be the most flavorful flesh (though in romaji, the kanji for "reply" has another extended "u" vowel as "ou" instead of an "o", making it "outourou"). In the English adaptations, this is called Tower Climb Return . Hirameki ( 閃 , Hirameki , literally meaning "Flash") : Two air-based projectiles are sent forward after swinging both swords from the left or right side. The name comes from "Hirame", a flat fish used for sushi. This was first seen being used against Kaku. In the English adaptations, this is called Flash . Samon ( 砂紋 , Samon , literally meaning "Sand Drawing") : Two air-based projectiles are sent diagonally down and to the left or right after swinging both swords from the left or right. The pun in this is obvious; it sounds like "Salmon", a common fish used for sushi. This was first seen being used against Kaku. In the English adaptations, this is called Ripple . In One Piece: Great Pirate Colosseum , this is merely used as one of Zoro's jumping attacks without the Nigiri stance being involved. Maguma ( 魔熊 , Maguma , literally meaning "Demon Bear") : A simple downward pound into the opponent with both swords. This was first seen being used against Ryuma . The name is derived from "Maguro", the Japanese name for tuna, another fish used for sushi and may be a pun on the Japanese pronunciation of "Magma". This is called Demon Bear in the VIZ manga and Funimation dub and Demon Magma Bear in the Funimation subs. Dai Gekken ( 大撃剣 , Dai Gekken , literally meaning "Great Fencing Sword") : Zoro pierces two of his swords into Franky 's heavy nunchaku and hits the opponent with it. This was first seen being used against Oars . This is called Great Shock Slash in the VIZ manga and Funimation dub and Super Fencing in the Funimation subs. Gekken literally means "striking/attack sword," which is normally a Japanese term associated with fencing. After the Timeskip This is a list showing the attacks Zoro used for his Two Sword Style after his two years of training with Dracule Mihawk . Most of the techniques listed are enhanced versions of his old attacks, save for a few exceptions. Nigiri: Toro Samon ( 弐斬り 登楼砂紋 , Nigiri: Tōrō Samon , literally meaning "Two Cutter: Climbing Tower Sand Drawing") : A combination of two former techniques, Toro and Samon , performed from his Nigiri stance. Zoro uses two swords in an upward diagonal slash to send a double air-based projectile attack that cuts his opponent. This was first seen being used against Basil Hawkins . The pun is that Nigiri is a type of sushi, Toro means fatty, and Samon is a homophone for Salmon in Japanese. Put it together and it is Fatty Salmon Sushi. In the English adaptations, this is called Nigiri Tower Climb Ripple . Nanahyakuniju Pound Ho ( 七百二十 煩悩 鳳 , Nanahyakunijū Pondo Hō , kanji meaning "Seven-Hundred Twenty Klesha Phoenix"; furigana meaning "720 Pound Phoenix/Cannon") : A more powerful version of the Nanajuni Pound Ho . Holding his two swords horizontally above the shoulder, Zoro then performs a circular swing that launches two air compressed projectiles spiraling towards the target. The attack was first seen being used against Kurozumi Orochi , though it was deflected by Kyoshiro . This is called 720-Pound Phoenix in the VIZ manga and Funimation subs and 720 Caliber Phoenix in the Funimation dub. Clear Lance ( 空狸槍 , Kuria Ransu , literally meaning "Sky Tanuki Spear") : Zoro performs a downward slash with two swords, generating a strong shockwave. The shockwave generated is such that it can be used while in midair to stop himself from falling and push himself a great distance, similar to Geppo or Sanji 's Sky Walk . It was first seen used during his fight against King to avoid falling from Onigashima, allowing him to return safely. This attack's name is a pun, as it sounds akin to the word "clearance" if using Japanese phonetics. Gokuraku Anyo Rashomon ( 極楽安養 羅生門 , Gokuraku Anyō Rashōmon , literally meaning "Fostering of Sukhavati, Rashomon/Gauze Life Gate") : After launching himself (or being launched) towards an opponent, Zoro cuts into them using his two handheld swords, held crosswise, while presumably moving past them in the process. The attack was first seen being used against one of the Five Elders , Ethanbaron V. Nusjuro , the latter blocking the attack with his Kitetsu blade, the resultant clash of Haki causing both men to be blown back. The Japanese term gokuraku ( 極楽 , lit. "extreme bliss") refers to Sukhavati , the Western Pure Land of the Amida Buddha in Mahayana Buddhism; anyō ( 安養 ) is based on a Chinese word for care provided for the elderly, a possible nod to Zoro using the attack on one of the Five Elders. In the English adaptations, this is called Restful Paradise Rashomon .

The Guru Guru no Mi is a Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to transform any part of their body or piece of worn clothing into propellers capable of rotation at high speed, becoming a Rotation Human ( 回転人間 , Kaiten Ningen , Funimation: "Fan Man") . It was eaten by Buffalo . Etymology "Guruguru" ( ぐるぐる/グルグル ) is a Japanese onomatopoeia that means (turning) round and round/in circles (or wrapping/winding around). In the VIZ Manga and Funimation adaptations, it is called the Spin-Spin Fruit . Strengths and Weaknesses The Guru Guru no Mi grants its eater the power to transform any part of their body or piece of worn clothing into propellers capable of rotation at high speed. The force of the spinning is strong enough to enable propelled flight by the user over very long distances, to assist in the carrying or thrown launch of objects and other people, and to create powerful windstorms. A person of sufficient strength can grab onto the user's rotational parts and stop the spinning, and can even do damage by twisting the parts themselves. Besides that, the user is also vulnerable to the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Buffalo mastered the Guru Guru no Mi to the point where he can turn his whole body into a mode of transportation. Even though Buffalo usually assumes a specific pose when he flies, he is still capable of moving his body freely when needed while still remaining midair. While on the ground, he can rapidly rotate his legs in a vertical fashion, allowing him to use them like roller skates. He also uses his power to execute team-attacks with his partner Baby 5 , who uses her Buki Buki no Mi powers. Techniques Toppu: Matasaburo ( 突風 又三郎 , Toppū: Matasaburō , literally meaning "Gust: Matasaburo") : Buffalo spins around to create a gust of wind powerful enough to disperse Shinokuni . Matasaburo is a character from Kenji Miyazawa 's story "Matasaburo of the Wind" ( 風の又三郎 , Kaze no Matasaburō ) , who can control and ride on wind. This is called Gust - Matasaburo in the VIZ manga and Funimation subs and Storm Gust - Matasaburo in the Funimation dub. Guru Guru Toshaho ( グルグル投射砲 , Guru Guru Tōshahō , literally meaning "Spin Spin Projectile Cannon") : After Baby 5 transforms into a large missile with her abilities, Buffalo spins his arms around his body to throw her against the enemy. The momentum and speed gained from the spinning, combined with Baby 5's ability to move in midair to dodge incoming enemy fire, makes this technique very hard to avoid and very powerful. It was used for the first time against Franky in his General Franky . This is called Spin-Spin Projectile Cannon in the VIZ manga and Funimation subs and Spin Projectile Cannon in the Funimation dub. History Buffalo ate the Guru Guru no Mi at no older than age 17, as he was seen using its powers above the Birdcage on Minion Island .

Scopper Gaban told him. In the anime, Zoro's first usage of Supreme King Haki occurs much earlier; while blending in with a group of Beasts Pirates, enraged over their mockery of the suffering of the residents of Ebisu Town and Okobore Town, he releases a burst of Haki, knocking several out, with him simply attributing their fainting to drinking too much fine liquor. Physical Abilities Noted to be the strongest of the Three Sweet Commanders, Katakuri possesses immense physical strength, being able to flick jellybeans with such extreme force and velocity that they act like bullets, mortally wounding his opponents with a single shot. Even from a great distance, Katakuri can kill a man with a flicked jellybean. He was also able to defeat Vinsmoke Ichiji, a genetically enhanced Germa 66 commander who

The Bata Bata no Mi is a Paramecia -type Devil Fruit which allows the user to create and control butter, making them a Butter Human ( バター人間 , Batā Ningen , VIZ: "Butterwoman") . It was eaten by Charlotte Galette . Etymology Bata comes from batā ( バター ) , the Japanese pronunciation of "butter". Strengths and Weaknesses This fruit allows the user to control seemingly limitless amounts of butter. Despite butter usually making objects slippery, the created butter has been shown to be quite sticky and adhesive when in semi-liquid form, capable of immobilizing an opponent by wrapping it around their hands or bodies. This fruit has shown no particular weaknesses aside from the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Galette uses this fruit's power in battle to immobilize and subdue her opponents, leaving them open for her allies to do as they please with them. How exactly she does this has not been shown in the manga, but in the anime she's been seen telekinetically controlling globs of butter and wrapping them around her opponent's hands and bodies.

Three Sword Style Two Sword Style One Sword Style No Sword Style Nine Sword Style Team Combinations Anime Only Video Game Only Video Game Only In the several One Piece video games, Zoro was given several techniques and moves, the known named techniques that are exclusive to the video games are as follows: Ittoryu: Koma Giri ( 狛斬 り , Koma Giri , literally meaning "Lion-Dog Cutter") : A downwards cut with a single blade. Used as a grounded-string attack starter in Grand Battle! Swan Colosseum . Ittoryu: Tobikkiri ( 鳶斬 り , Koma Giri , literally meaning "Black Kite Cutter") : A midair version of Koma Giri in Grand Battle! Swan Colosseum . Ittoryu: Shishito ( 獅子刀 , Shishitō , literally meaning "Lion Blade") : A standard diagonal or sideways cut, mainly serves to make a combo to another attacks. Shishitou is also a name of a Japanese species of sweet green pepper. Presented in Grand Battle! , Grand Battle! 2 , One Piece Grand Battle 3 , and One Piece: Grand Battle! Rush! ; used as his front attack string starter especially in both Rush! and One Piece: Grand Adventure where it is localized as Single Sword "Lion Blade" . Nitoryu: Takitate ( 滝断 , Takitate , literally meaning "Cascade Sever") : Zoro crosses two swords and makes a cut onward, usually follows Shishito. Takitate is a term for "freshly cooked" in Japanese. Takitate Oni Giri ( 滝断鬼斬り , Takitate Oni Giri , literally meaning "Cascade Severing Ogre Cutter") : An diagonally-upward version of Oni Giri used in Gigant Battle 2 , which is in turn a variation of the above technique. The pun is that the term can also translate to "freshly cooked oni giri/rice ball". Nitoryu: Hachimaki ( 蜂巻 , Hachimaki , literally meaning "Bee Twister") : Zoro thrusts to the air with two of his swords. Hachimaki can also mean headband. Presented in Grand Battl e and Grand Battle 2 . Ittoryu: Nezumizan ( 鼠斬 , Nezumizan , literally meaning "Mouse/Rat Slasher") : Zoro slashes out a grounded wave forward as a projectile, though it is not the same as Taka Nami or Yakkodori . Nezumizan is also a term that means exponential progression or proliferation if read as 鼠算 (can also literally mean "mouse mathematics"). Presented in Grand Battle and Grand Battle 2 , but also done as a single-bladed outward slash followup to Koma Giri in Grand Battle! Swan Colosseum without it firing a projectile. Nitoryu: Kama Kiri ( 鎌斬り , Kama Kiri , literally meaning "Sickle Cutter") : While airborne, Zoro thrusts down diagonally, Kamakiri is the Japanese name for praying mantis. Presented in Grand Battle and Grand Battle 2 . Ittoryu: Kumanbachi ( 熊罰 , Kumanbachi , literally meaning "Bear Punishment") : While airborne, Zoro descends down with his sword positioned to stab. It is similar to Cabaji's Ichirin-Zashi and also to Zoro's Maguma even though the latter was used after the games were released. Kumanbachi is also the name for the Japanese species of hornet. Presented in all Grand Battle series as one of his airborne moves, and is localized as Single Sword "Bear Punisher" in both Grand Battle Rush and Grand Adventure . Ittoryu: Kushizashi ( 串刺 し , Kushizashi , literally meaning "Skewer Thorn") : While airborne, Zoro dives down with a single blade for a fierce sword-plant that also emits a smoke tremor upon impact with the ground. Used as his midair downwards special attack in Battle Stadium D.O.N . Nitoryu: Nihonshu ( 二本狩 , Nihonshu , literally meaning "Two Point Hunts") : Zoro dashes forward then performs a cross cut with 2 swords. Nihonshu is also another name for Japanese sake. Presented in Grand Battle , Grand Battle 2 and Grand Battle! Swan Colosseum . Nitoryu: Togyu ( 刀牛 , Tōgyū , literally meaning "Blade Bull") : Zoro grabs his opponent by pinching with his 2 swords then throws the opponent behind. Togyu also means bullfight (or literally fighting bull). Presented in Grand Battle and Grand Battle 2 . Ittoryu: Togyu Barai ( 刃千払い , Tōgyū Barai , literally meaning "Blade Bull Sweep") : An improved version of the above technique as an Ittoryu technique, where Zoro skewers someone on his sword to toss them away behind himself. Used as his basic throw attack in Gigant Battle 2 . Ittoryu: Bato ( 馬刀 , Batō , literally meaning "Horse Blade") : A cut downward with single sword, follows Shisito in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle RUSH as his front attack string's second input, and is known as Single Sword: Bronco Blade in the localization. Bato is Japanese for abuse, but can also mean "horsehead" and as the Japanese term "Batō/Mezu Kannon" for Hayagriva, a tantric Buddhist deity. Ittoryu: Bento ( 弁刀 , Bentō , literally meaning "Valve Blade") : A standard two-handed lifting-scoop slash that sends the opponent flying upward to start an aerial combo. Bento is a traditional japanese lunch box. Presented in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle Rush as his upper attack string starting input. Localized as Single Sword "Valve Blade" . Nitoryu: Suito ( 水刀 , Suitō , literally meaning "Water Blade") : Follows Bento, Zoro crosses his swords and slashes upwards. Suito also means "water bottle", "flask" or "canteen", which usually accompanies a lunch box. Presented in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle Rush as his upper attack string second input, and localized as Dual Sword "Water Blade" . Santoryu: Kogarashi ( 虎狩 らし , Kogarashi , literally meaning "Tiger Hunter") : After sending opponent to the air, Zoro rises into the air with a spinning slash upward, similar to a combination of both Tatsu Maki and Sai Kuru . Kogarashi can also means cold wintry wind, a unique climate in Japan. Presented in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle Rush as his upper attack string final input, and localized as Triple Sword "Tiger Hunt" (and is not to be confused with Tora Gari ). Santoryu: Maku no Uchi ( 幕 ノ 討 ち , Maku no Uchi , literally meaning "Curtain's Conquer") : Zoro leaps in the air and tumbles with a triple-blade slash to slam the opponent to the ground. Makunouchi is a type of lunchbox which consists of fish, meat, pickles, eggs and vegetables along with rice and an umeboshi. Presented in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle Rush as one of his upper attack string ending inputs; localized as Triple Sword "Final Curtain" . Ittoryu: Issen ( 一閃 , Issen , literally meaning "One Flash/Brandish") : While airborne, Zoro descends diagonally and slashes with single sword. Issen also means battle when written in a different kanji. Presented in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle Rush as his midair front + upper attack input. Localized as Single Sword "Sword Flash" . Ittoryu Iai: Issen ( 一刀流 ｢居合｣ 一閃 , Ittōryū Iai: Issen , literally meaning "One Sword/Blade Style Acting-Draw: One Flash/Brandish") : A stronger version of the above technique seen in Gigant Battle 2 , as one of Zoro's special moves, where Zoro slightly steps forward and slashes outward with a single wide slash that knocks back, though it is more akin to a swatting-battoujutsu slash than a quick-draw-styled re-sheath as the name implies (even though Zoro does re-sheath his sword afterwards). Nitoryu: Sento ( 閃刀 , Sentō , literally meaning "Flash Blade") : Much like Togyu, Zoro grabs his opponent by pinching with his 2 swords then throws the opponent upward to slash him. Sento is a japanese public bath. Presented in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle Rush as his direction + throw, and localized as Dual Sword "Flash Blade" . Nitoryu: Sento Gayoi ( 閃刀 ・ 牙酔 , Sentō Gayoi , literally meaning "Flash Blade: Drunk Fang") : A version of Sento with Zoro slashing the opponent to the back. " Sento ga yoi " is a phrase that means "Go to public bath". Presented in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle Rush as Zoro's basic throw attack, and is localized as Dual Sword "Flash Fang" . Santoryu: Sanjuso ( 三獣奏 , Sanjūsō , literally meaning "Three Beast Performance/Player") : A special combo composed of Gazami Dori , Tatsu Maki and Ushi Bari . Sanjuso also a term that means musical trio. Presented in all Grand Battle games as one of Zoro's super moves (each game has him perform the combination in a varying order), and is localized as Triple Sword "Tri-Beast Requiem" . Kokyu no Dento ( 呼吸 の 伝刀 , Kokyū no Dentō , literally meaning "Respiration's Transfer Blade") : Zoro points his sword forward, causing an aura to emit from it which inflicts damage then striking a pose that emits a small shockwave; which grants a buff that makes his attacks (save for his 36 Pound Cannon , Sanjuso and Toro Nagashi ) inflict more damage multiplied by 1.8 for a set duration. However, for the said duration, Zoro's ability to block is also disabled in tandem, but the move can be input again to turn off the buff. Presented in Grand Battle 3 and Grand Battle Rush , where it is one of his front attack string second inputs and is localized as Breath of Sword . Oddly enough, this technique by 19 years, predates Zoro's eventual usage of the En'o Santoryu where akin to the naming of Kokyu no Dento, he charges his blades with his own breath. Ogi! San Zen Ryusen !! ( 奥義！三千龍閃！！ , Ōgi! Sansen Ryūsen!! , literally meaning "Secret Skill! Three Thousand Dragons Flash!!) : Only present in the game Jump Super Stars , Combination attack that involves Zoro teaming up with Himura Kenshin from the manga Rurouni Kenshin, where Zoro performs Sanzen Sekai combined with Kenshin's Kuzuryuusen (九頭龍閃, Nine-Headed Dragon Flash). Butta Giri ( 仏陀斬り , Butta Giri , literally meaning "Buddha Steep Cutter") : A technique seen in Gigant Battle 2 , where Zoro charges up for a single-bladed quick-draw re-sheath (with Sandai Kitetsu drawn from behind as Zoro kneels forward) akin to Shi Shishi Sonson where he flash-dashes forward for a cut. Performs this instead of Tora Gari when the basic attack button is held down for long enough (for about 1 second total, which also allows Zoro to shift his directional facing). The naming is a pun off of "buttagiri/buttagiru" (a combination of "hitting" and "slicing"), which means "to chop". Santoryu: Urabon'ei ( 裏煩影 , Urabon'ei , literally meaning "Reverse Anxious/Ill Shadow") : A technique seen in Gigant Battle 2 , where Zoro slashes downward with a dashing-slash past his target(s), also slashing with his other swords akin to a diagonally-aimed and/or vertical-version of Oni Giri . Used as a combo finishing flank slash after slashing through his target(s) with Rashomon (though it will do less damage if does not pass through). The naming is a pun off of "urabon'e" (without the ending "i" vowel), the Japanese term for the Bon Festival; the first and last kanji often vary (either being 于 or 盂 for the former and either 会 or 會 for the latter) while the rest stay consistent in the original term. Santoryu: Shoronpo ( 昇龍砲 , Shōronpō , literally meaning "Rising Dragon Cannon") : A followup to Tatsu Maki akin to an improved version of Kogarashi (and the airborne spin Zoro uses to prop-up for the original airborne version of Shi Shishi Sonson ), where Zoro spins into the air with his blades in a small wind. The pun is that shoronpo also the Japanese way to read "xiaolongbao" (lit. small basket buns), a popular type of Chinese dumpling. Used in Gigant Battle 2 . Nitouryu: Senryu ( 閃龍 , Senryū , literally meaning "Flash Dragon") : A followup to his aforementioned Shoronpo , where he slashes to his side with both swords as he leans to his side, akin to an airborne version of Hirameki and Nigorizake , but without leaning to the side. Used in Gigant Battle 2 . Ittoryu: Shoro Nagashi ( 鐘楼流し , Shōrō Nagashi , literally meaning "Belfry Stream/Flowing") : An enhanced version of Toro Nagashi used in both Gigant Battle 2 and Super Grand Battle! X . Zoro slightly unsheathes his swords at the side, allowing him to dodge an incoming attack with a counter slash via a standing spin into a swat-slash. Akin to its parent technique, Shoro Nagashi is a play on another "nagashi" term, known as "shourounagashi" or "shouryounagashi" (精霊流し, lit. deceased spirit flowing), the part of the aforementioned Bon Festival where boats are sent off in ode to the deceased. Nitoryu: Yakkodori: Suiren ( 厄港鳥 粋連 , Yakkōdori: Suiren , literally meaning "Disaster Harbor Bird: Pith Chain") : A Nitoryu variation of Yakkodori used in Gigant Battle 2 , where Zoro unleashes two crossed-waves at once when the attack button is held down and released for almost half a second (holding it down further charges it up for him to release it as a 180 Pound Cannon instead). "Suiren" can also mean "drowsy lotus" (the Japanese term for "water lily") or "water refinement" (the Japanese term for "swimming practice"). Nitoryu: Ikura ( 猪蔵 , Ikura , literally meaning "Boar Cellar") : A sideways-forward slash with both swords in midair that moves Zoro forwards a short distance, with the motion done akin to Nigori-Zake (or is essentially an alternate midair variant). Used as a basic midair attack in Gigant Battle 2 . "Ikura" is a term for "salted salmon roe" (mainly pertaining to the individual mature eggs), or to say "how much/amount".

The Riba Riba no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to reverse time around them, making the user a Reverse Human ( リバース人間 , Ribāsu Ningen ) . It was eaten by Snitcher . Etymology "Riba" is derived from ribāsu ( リバース ) , the Japanese way of pronouncing the word "reverse". Strengths and Weaknesses The Riba Riba no Mi grants the user the ability to reverse the movements of others or the flow of energy, including physical force. They have the ability to selectively enforce this reversal, applying only to specific targets, or to apply it over all eligible targets in a given area. The reversal of a person's movements additionally leaves them disoriented for a few seconds, and the reversal of energy allows the user to functionally reflect attacks based onto their user. For the reversal ability to function, it must be manually activated by the user; it cannot be set to work automatically. This means that the user can only reflect attacks they are able to react to, and leaves them vulnerable if they are distracted. No other weaknesses are known outside the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Snitcher uses the Riba Riba no Mi to reverse the movements and energy of those around him, typically for combat or capture purposes. In combat, he frequently reverses the attacks of his opponents, leaving him nigh-untouchable in single combat. Additionally, he uses movement reversal to leave targets disoriented, allowing them to be easily captured, even by his weaker subordinates. Techniques Reverse ( リバース , Ribāsu ) : The Riba Riba no Mi's primary technique, the user makes a circle motion with their arm, reversing time on an intended target. This technique is accompanied by a flash of light lasting as long as the reversal is kept active, typically purple, green, or blue.

ability_name: Zou Zou no Mi, Model: Mammoth
ability_description: For other uses, see Zou (Disambiguation) . The Zou Zou no Mi, Model: Mammoth is an Ancient Zoan -type Devil Fruit , which allows the user to transform into a mammoth hybrid and full mammoth at will. It was eaten by Jack . Etymology "Zou" ( 象 , Zō ) means "elephant". Strengths and Weaknesses This fruit allows the user to transform in full or hybrid mammoth at will. The full mammoth form granted by this fruit gives the user a massive frame, significantly increasing Jack's already abnormal size. As an Ancient Zoan, this fruit grants the user greater strength than a normal Zoan, Jack can level buildings with a single swing of his trunk and leave imprints of his feet on the ground just by stepping on it. The hybrid form grants the user a centaur-like body with four mammoth legs, a peculiar case for Zoan users, with the upper body of a man with the head, trunk, and tusks of a mammoth. In this form, the user is smaller but still large in size, as well as being faster and capable of wielding weapons for combat. However, the user is extremely heavy and slow in their full beast form, leaving them unable to dodge high-speed attacks. Otherwise, the user is affected by the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Jack has used this fruit to improve his combat capabilities; he can cause massive destruction with a single attack and bulldoze nearly anything that gets in his way by charging forward. So far, his only displayed attack has been swinging his trunk to damage his opponents and surroundings. In his hybrid form, however, Jack can wield both his shotel blades and use his Devil Fruit powers to augment the power of his swordsmanship in battle. Due to being a fish-man , Jack can remain conscious and breathe underwater for an indefinite amount of time, although remaining unable to move in the meanwhile. History The Zou Zou no Mi, Model Mammoth was eaten less than 20 years ago by Jack .

Haki Armament Haki Marco is a highly skilled user of Armament Haki, shown by his ability to imbue his legs with it to physically contact and kick away Logia users such as Marine Admirals Kizaru and Aokiji. He, alongside Vista, used this type of Haki against Admiral Akainu, although it had zero effect on him. Observation Haki Marco can use Observation Haki. During the Raid on Onigashima, he was able

An advanced level of Observation Haki allows the user to actually foresee a short period into the future. , which allows the user to use their own aura as armor to defend against attacks, as well as make their own attacks more potent. It can also be used to bypass Devil Fruit defenses, such as the intangibility of a Logia. A person can apply the armament to a section of their body, over their entire body, and even imbue it onto their weapons. A coating of Armament Haki will cause the coated area to turn a shiny black, and a particularly thick and powerful coating will have a flame-like pattern along its edge.

The Chuni Chuni no Mi is a non-canon Paramecia -type Devil Fruit that allows the user to "tune" abilities and powers, making the user a Tuning Human ( チューニング人間 , Chūningu Ningen ) . It was eaten by Yehudi . Etymology "Chuni" is derived from chūningu ( チューニング ) , the Japanese way of pronouncing the word "tuning". Strengths and Weaknesses The main strength of this fruit is that it allows the user to tune, i.e. increase or decrease the abilities and powers of people by firing beams known as Age Age Beams ( アゲアゲビーム , Age Age Bīmu , from age meaning "increase") or Sage Sage Beams ( サゲサゲビーム , Sage Sage Bīmu , from sage meaning "decrease") In addition, it is possible to make finer and stronger adjustments by touching the target directly. The user can also adjust their own power as well as the environment. Those affected by one of the beams will have an up or down arrow on the back. Additionally, touching someone and using the fruit's abilities greatly boosts its strength. This boost in strength was enough to bring Shirotsu from unable to harm Luffy to being capable of seriously fighting him. The tuning of the Chuni Chuni no Mi is also not limited to living beings, as Yehudi was capable of manipulating the energy of a volcano. The effects of the Chuni Chuni no Mi can be washed off with water. No other weaknesses are known outside the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage Yehudi uses this ability to increase the strength of himself and his allies and decrease the power of his opponents. Techniques Gokigen Tuning ( ゴキゲンチューニング , Gokigen Chūningu ) : A technique that enhances strength. Gokigen ( ご機嫌 ) means "in good spirits" in Japanese. Gennari Tuning ( ゲンナリチューニング , Gennari Chūningu ) : A technique that randomly limits the opponent's abilities. Gennari ( げんなり ) means "worn out" or "dispirited" in Japanese. When used on Alvida , Galdino , and Buggy , it stopped Alvida from being slippery , Galdino from being able to raise a wax wall , and Buggy from separating his body . When used on Luffy , it stopped him from stretching , and when used on Zoro , it drastically reduced his physical strength. Age Age Morimori Direct Tuning ( アゲアゲモリモリダイレクトチューニング , Age Age Morimori Dairekuto Chūningu ) : While touching his target, Yehudi grants them a massive strength buff. This ability was first used to power up Shirotsu . Morimori Direct Gokigen Tuning ( モリモリダイレクトゴキゲンチューニング , Morimori Dairekuto Gokigen Chūningu ) : The reverse of Age Age Morimori Direct Tuning , Yehudi touches his target and absorbs the strength he granted them back into himself. This ability was first used to absorb the power Yehudi had given to Shirotsu in preparation for his fight against Luffy. Age Age Age Age Age Age Grand Inferno ( アゲアゲアゲアゲアゲアゲグランド・インフェルノ , Age Age Age Age Age Age Gurando Inferuno ) : Yehudi tunes the energy of Soil Island 's volcano, causing magma and smoke to burst onto the surface. Lighting Shot : Lighting Buster : The final attack Yehudi used before being defeated by Luffy and Zoro. It is unclear what it actually does, as Yehudi was defeated while using it.

ability_name: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
ability_description: For other models of this fruit, see Tori Tori no Mi (Disambiguation) . The Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon is a Zoan -type Devil Fruit that allows its user to transform into a falcon hybrid and a full falcon at will. It was eaten by Pell , and according to him, it is one of the only five types of flying powers ever discovered. Etymology Tori ( 鳥 ) is Japanese for "bird". While the kanji is written as 隼 ( hayabusa , Japanese for "falcon") , the pronunciation remains as farukon , the Japanese way to pronounce the species' name. In the 4Kids dub this fruit is simply known as the Tweet-Tweet Fruit . See below for more information. The VIZ Manga refers this fruit as the Tweet-Tweet Fruit, Falcon Type . The Funimation adaptations calls this fruit the Bird-Bird Fruit Model: The Falcon . Strengths and Weaknesses This fruit enables a person to become a large falcon and a falcon-human hybrid at will, granting him the enhanced senses of a falcon, along with the advantage of having one of the only few Devil Fruits that allow flight. In his full falcon form, he can carry other people on his back while flying. Also in this form, weight does not seem to be much of an issue, as he carried a massive bomb high into the air without showing any strain. This fruit does not seem to have any specific weaknesses, aside from the standard Devil Fruit weaknesses. Usage As shown by Pell, this Devil Fruit grants him increased speed and strength, as well as the ability to use his talons to slash through his opponents. Being a predatory bird, Pell is better suited for physical combat than standard Zoan users. It also allows him to fly. This is useful in both traveling and attacking. He is seen attacking with a pair of Gatling guns, making him resemble a fighter plane. He also gave other people rides on his back in his full falcon form. Techniques Tobizume ( 飛爪 , Tobizume , literally meaning "Flying Talon") : Pell takes flight in his human/falcon hybrid form, swoops down on his opponent, and attacks with his talons. When using this attack, Pell flies at a blinding speed, appearing to be invisible. This was first used against a group of pirates. In the VIZ manga and 4Kids dub, this is called Flying Claws . In the Funimation adaptations this is called Flying Talons . Translation Issues The 4Kids name for the fruit, Tweet Tweet Fruit, has a similar issue as the "Mutt Mutt" Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal and Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Dachshund , where the name is indirectly connected to the animal. While the sound is associated with smaller birds, it strongly contradicts the violent and predatory nature of a falcon. Furthermore, "tweet" is a stereotypical bird call, and most birds do not use a "tweet"-like call.

Yet another way to counter predictive Observation Haki is by simply being so fast that, even if an enemy can predict one's attack, they cannot dodge in time. This was shown when Luffy used his Gear 2 against Boa Sandersonia and Boa Marigold, where even though Sandersonia was reading his movements, Luffy's speed was too high for her to keep up with, which made her prediction ineffective. The foresight of advanced Observation Haki when mastered. The foresight of advanced Observation Haki when awakened.
